title: RTMC: Step-Level Credit Assignment via Rollout Trees
authors: Tao Wang, Suhang Zheng, Xiaoxiao Xu
abstract: Multi-step agentic reinforcement learning benefits from fine-grained credit assignment, yet existing approaches offer limited options: critic-free methods like GRPO assign a uniform advantage to every action in a trajectory, while learned value networks introduce notable overhead and can be fragile under sparse rewards. We observe that group rollouts targeting the same problem often traverse overlapping intermediate states, implicitly forming a tree whose branches diverge at successive decision points. Building on this insight, we introduce Rollout-Tree Monte Carlo (RTMC) advantage estimation, which aggregates return statistics across rollouts sharing a common state to produce per-step Q-values and advantages--without any learned critic. A state-action signature system compresses raw interaction histories into compact, comparable representations, making cross-rollout state matching tractable. On SWE-bench Verified, RTMC improves pass@1 by 3.2 percentage points over GRPO.
title_zh: RTMC：通过回溯树实现逐级信用分配
abstract_zh: 多步代理强化学习得益于细粒度的信用分配，但现有方法选择有限：无评判器的方法（如GRPO）对轨迹中的每个动作赋予相同的优势值，而基于学习的价值网络则引入显著开销，并在稀疏奖励环境下容易变得脆弱。我们观察到，针对同一问题的分组滚动采样往往经过重叠的中间状态，隐式形成一棵树状结构，其分支在连续决策点处分离。基于这一洞察，我们提出了滚动树蒙特卡洛（RTMC）优势估计方法，通过聚合共享相同状态的多个滚动采样返回统计信息，无需任何学习型评判器即可生成每一步的Q值和优势值。一种状态-动作签名系统将原始交互历史压缩为紧凑且可比较的表示，使跨滚动采样间的状态匹配成为可能。在SWE-bench Verified基准上，RTMC相较于GRPO将pass@1指标提升了3.2个百分点。

title: ArtiCAD: Articulated CAD Assembly Design via Multi-Agent Code Generation
authors: Yuan Shui, Yandong Guan, Zhanwei Zhang, Juncheng Hu, Jing Zhang, Dong Xu, Qian Yu
abstract: Parametric Computer-Aided Design (CAD) of articulated assemblies is essential for product development, yet generating these multi-part, movable models from high-level descriptions remains unexplored. To address this, we propose ArtiCAD, the first training-free multi-agent system capable of generating editable, articulated CAD assemblies directly from text or images. Our system divides this complex task among four specialized agents: Design, Generation, Assembly, and Review. One of our key insights is to predict assembly relationships during the initial design stage rather than the assembly stage. By utilizing a Connector that explicitly defines attachment points and joint parameters, ArtiCAD determines these relationships before geometry generation, effectively bypassing the limited spatial reasoning capabilities of current LLMs and VLMs. To further ensure high-quality outputs, we introduce validation steps in the generation and assembly stages, accompanied by a cross-stage rollback mechanism that accurately isolates and corrects design- and code-level errors. Additionally, a self-evolving experience store accumulates design knowledge to continuously improve performance on future tasks. Extensive evaluations on three datasets (ArtiCAD-Bench, CADPrompt, and ACD) validate the effectiveness of our approach. We further demonstrate the applicability of ArtiCAD in requirement-driven conceptual design, physical prototyping, and the generation of embodied AI training assets through URDF export.
title_zh: ArtiCAD：基于多智能体代码生成的可动CAD装配设计
abstract_zh: 参数化计算机辅助设计（CAD）在可动组件装配体的开发中至关重要，然而如何从高层次描述自动生成多部件、可移动的模型，仍是一个未被充分探索的领域。为解决这一问题，我们提出了ArtiCAD——首个无需训练的多智能体系统，能够直接根据文本或图像生成可编辑、可动的CAD装配体。我们的系统将这一复杂任务分解为四个专业智能体：设计（Design）、生成（Generation）、装配（Assembly）和评审（Review）。一个关键洞察是：在初始设计阶段就预测装配关系，而非在装配阶段进行。通过引入“连接器”（Connector），明确定义连接点与关节参数，ArtiCAD在几何生成之前即确定装配关系，从而有效规避了当前大语言模型（LLMs）和视觉语言模型（VLMs）在空间推理能力上的局限性。为进一步确保输出质量，我们在生成与装配阶段引入验证步骤，并配备跨阶段回滚机制，精准定位并修复设计与代码层面的错误。此外，系统还配备一个自我演进的经验存储库，持续积累设计知识，以提升未来任务的表现。在三个数据集（ArtiCAD-Bench、CADPrompt 和 ACD）上的大量评估验证了本方法的有效性。我们进一步展示了ArtiCAD在需求驱动的概念设计、物理原型制作以及生成具身人工智能训练资源（通过URDF导出）方面的广泛应用潜力。

title: HTAA: Enhancing LLM Planning via Hybrid Toolset Agentization & Adaptation
authors: Chengrui Huang, Junshuo Zhang, Zhiyuan Ma, Xikun Wang, Ximeng Wang, Menghua Jiang, Gang Zeng, Zhaobing Han, Shen Gao, Shuo Shang
abstract: Enabling large language models to scale and reliably use hundreds of tools is critical for real-world applications, yet challenging due to the inefficiency and error accumulation inherent in flat tool-calling architectures. To address this, we propose Hybrid Toolset Agentization & Adaptation (HTAA), a hierarchical framework for scalable tool-use planning. We propose a novel toolset agentization paradigm, which encapsulates frequently co-used tools into specialized agent tools, thereby reducing the planner's action space and mitigating redundancy. To ensure effective coordination, we design Asymmetric Planner Adaptation, a trajectory-based training paradigm that aligns the high-level planner with agent tools via backward reconstruction and forward refinement. To validate the performance of HTAA, we conduct experiments on a real-world internal dataset, InfoVerify, based on the POI validation workflow of China's largest online large-scale ride-hailing platform, featuring long-horizon executable tool trajectories. Experiments on InfoVerify and widely-used benchmarks show that HTAA consistently achieves higher task success rates, requires short tool calling trajectories, and significantly reduces context overhead compared to strong baselines. Furthermore, in a production deployment, HTAA substantially reduces manual validation effort and operational cost, demonstrating its practical efficacy.
title_zh: HTAA：通过混合工具集代理化与适应性增强大模型规划
abstract_zh: 使大型语言模型能够规模化、可靠地使用数百种工具，对于现实世界的应用至关重要，但这一目标因扁平化工具调用架构固有的低效性与错误累积问题而面临挑战。为解决该问题，我们提出了一种分层框架——混合工具集代理化与自适应机制（Hybrid Toolset Agentization & Adaptation, HTAA），以实现可扩展的工具使用规划。我们引入了一种新颖的工具集代理化范式，将频繁协同使用的工具封装为专用的代理工具，从而缩小规划器的动作空间，有效减少冗余。为确保高效协调，我们设计了非对称规划器自适应机制（Asymmetric Planner Adaptation），这是一种基于轨迹的训练范式，通过反向重构与正向优化，使高层规划器与代理工具之间实现对齐。为验证HTAA的性能，我们在一个真实世界内部数据集InfoVerify上进行了实验，该数据集基于中国最大在线大规模网约车平台的POI（兴趣点）验证工作流构建，包含长时程可执行的工具调用轨迹。在InfoVerify及广泛使用的基准测试上的实验结果表明，HTAA在任务成功率方面持续优于强基线模型，所需工具调用轨迹更短，并显著降低了上下文开销。此外，在实际生产部署中，HTAA大幅减少了人工验证的工作量和运营成本，充分展现了其实际应用价值。

title: Polyglot Teachers: Evaluating Language Models for Multilingual Synthetic Data Generation
authors: Lester James V. Miranda, Ivan Vulić, Anna Korhonen
abstract: Synthesizing supervised finetuning (SFT) data from language models (LMs) to teach smaller models multilingual tasks has become increasingly common. However, teacher model selection is often ad hoc, typically defaulting to the largest available option, even though such models may have significant capability gaps in non-English languages. This practice can result in poor-quality synthetic data and suboptimal student downstream performance. In this work, we systematically characterize what makes an effective multilingual teacher. We measure intrinsic measures of data quality with extrinsic student model performance in a metric we call Polyglot Score; evaluating 10 LMs across 6 typologically diverse languages, generating over 1.4M SFT examples and training 240 student models. Among the models tested, Gemma 3 27B and Aya Expanse 32B emerge as consistently effective teachers across different student base model families. Further analyses reveal that model scale alone does not significantly predict teacher effectiveness; instead, data qualities such as prompt diversity, length, and response fluency capture over 93.3% of variance in intrinsic data quality and predict student performance. Finally, we provide practical recommendations, including matching the model families of teacher-student pairs and translating from or responding to existing prompts, which can yield improvements for less-resourced languages. We hope that our work advances data-centric research in multilingual synthetic data and LM development.
title_zh: 多语种教师：评估语言模型在多语言合成数据生成中的应用
abstract_zh: 从语言模型（LMs）中合成有监督微调（SFT）数据，以指导小型模型完成多语言任务的做法日益普遍。然而，教师模型的选择往往缺乏系统性，通常默认选择可用的最大模型，即使这些模型在非英语语言上的能力存在显著差距。这种做法可能导致合成数据质量低下，进而影响学生模型在下游任务中的表现。本文系统地分析了有效多语言教师模型的特征。我们通过“Polyglot Score”这一指标，将内在的数据质量度量与外在的学生模型性能相结合进行评估，在6种语言类型差异较大的语言上测试了10个语言模型，生成超过140万条SFT样本，并训练了240个学生模型。实验结果表明，Gemma 3 27B 和 Aya Expanse 32B 在不同学生基础模型家族中均表现出持续有效的教学能力。进一步分析显示，模型规模本身并不能显著预测教师的有效性；相反，提示多样性、长度以及响应流畅性等数据质量特征能够解释超过93.3%的内在数据质量方差，并有效预测学生模型的表现。最后，我们提出了若干实用建议，包括匹配教师与学生模型的架构家族，以及基于现有提示进行翻译或回应，这些方法对资源较少的语言能带来显著改进。我们希望本研究能推动多语言合成数据及语言模型发展的数据驱动型研究进展。

title: CodeTracer: Towards Traceable Agent States
authors: Han Li, Yifan Yao, Letian Zhu, Rili Feng, Hongyi Ye, Jiaming Wang, Yancheng He, Pengyu Zou, Lehan Zhang, Xinping Lei, Haoyang Huang, Ken Deng, Ming Sun, Zhaoxiang Zhang, He Ye, Jiaheng Liu
abstract: Code agents are advancing rapidly, but debugging them is becoming increasingly difficult. As frameworks orchestrate parallel tool calls and multi-stage workflows over complex tasks, making the agent's state transitions and error propagation hard to observe. In these runs, an early misstep can trap the agent in unproductive loops or even cascade into fundamental errors, forming hidden error chains that make it hard to tell when the agent goes off track and why. Existing agent tracing analyses either focus on simple interaction or rely on small-scale manual inspection, which limits their scalability and usefulness for real coding workflows. We present CodeTracer, a tracing architecture that parses heterogeneous run artifacts through evolving extractors, reconstructs the full state transition history as a hierarchical trace tree with persistent memory, and performs failure onset localization to pinpoint the failure origin and its downstream chain. To enable systematic evaluation, we construct CodeTraceBench from a large collection of executed trajectories generated by four widely used code agent frameworks on diverse code tasks (e.g., bug fixing, refactoring, and terminal interaction), with supervision at both the stage and step levels for failure localization. Experiments show that CodeTracer substantially outperforms direct prompting and lightweight baselines, and that replaying its diagnostic signals consistently recovers originally failed runs under matched budgets. Our code and data are publicly available.
title_zh: CodeTracer：面向可追溯的智能体状态
abstract_zh:
代码代理（Code Agents）正在迅速发展，但其调试却变得日益困难。随着框架在复杂任务中协调并行工具调用和多阶段工作流，代理的状态转换与错误传播过程难以观测。在这些运行过程中，早期的失误可能导致代理陷入无产出的循环，甚至引发根本性错误，形成隐蔽的错误链，使得难以判断代理何时偏离轨道以及原因何在。现有的代理追踪分析方法要么仅关注简单的交互模式，要么依赖小规模的人工检查，这限制了它们在真实编码工作流中的可扩展性和实用性。

我们提出了 CodeTracer——一种新型的追踪架构。该架构通过不断演进的提取器解析异构的运行产物，以持久化内存重建完整的状态转移历史，形成层次化的追踪树，并实现故障发生点的精确定位，从而识别出故障源头及其下游影响链。

为支持系统性评估，我们基于四个广泛使用的代码代理框架，在多样化的代码任务（如缺陷修复、重构、终端交互等）上生成大量已执行轨迹，构建了 CodeTraceBench 数据集，并在阶段和步骤两个层级提供标注信息，用于故障定位。实验表明，CodeTracer 显著优于直接提示（direct prompting）和轻量级基线方法；且在相同资源预算下，重放其诊断信号能够持续恢复原本失败的运行流程。我们的代码与数据均已公开。

title: SWE-AGILE: A Software Agent Framework for Efficiently Managing Dynamic Reasoning Context
authors: Shuquan Lian, Juncheng Liu, Yazhe Chen, Yuhong Chen, Hui Li
abstract: Prior representative ReAct-style approaches in autonomous Software Engineering (SWE) typically lack the explicit System-2 reasoning required for deep analysis and handling complex edge cases. While recent reasoning models demonstrate the potential of extended Chain-of-Thought (CoT), applying them to the multi-turn SWE task creates a fundamental dilemma: retaining full reasoning history leads to context explosion and ``Lost-in-the-Middle'' degradation, while discarding it would force the agent to redundantly re-reason at every step. To address these challenges, we propose SWE-AGILE, a novel software agent framework designed to bridge the gap between reasoning depth, efficiency, and context constraints. SWE-AGILE introduces a Dynamic Reasoning Context strategy, maintaining a ``sliding window'' of detailed reasoning for immediate continuity to prevent redundant re-analyzing, while compressing historical reasoning content into concise Reasoning Digests. Empirically, SWE-AGILE sets a new standard for 7B-8B models on SWE-Bench-Verified using only 2.2k trajectories and 896 tasks. Code is available at https://github.com/KDEGroup/SWE-AGILE.
title_zh: SWE-AGILE：一种用于高效管理动态推理上下文的软件代理框架
abstract_zh: 在自主软件工程（SWE）领域，以往的ReAct风格方法通常缺乏深度分析和处理复杂边缘情况所必需的显式系统2（System-2）推理能力。尽管近期的推理模型展示了扩展思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）的潜力，但在应用于多轮SWE任务时，却面临一个根本性困境：保留完整的推理历史会导致上下文爆炸和“中间迷失”（Lost-in-the-Middle）问题，而舍弃历史则迫使智能体在每一步都重复进行相同的推理，造成效率低下。为解决这些挑战，我们提出了SWE-AGILE——一种新型软件智能体框架，旨在弥合理论深度、执行效率与上下文约束之间的差距。SWE-AGILE引入了动态推理上下文机制，通过维护一个包含详细推理内容的“滑动窗口”，确保当前推理的连贯性，避免冗余分析；同时将历史推理信息压缩为简洁的推理摘要（Reasoning Digests）。实证研究表明，仅使用2.2k条轨迹和896个任务，SWE-AGILE即在SWE-Bench-Verified基准上为7B-8B规模的模型树立了新标准。代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/KDEGroup/SWE-AGILE。

title: Select Smarter, Not More: Prompt-Aware Evaluation Scheduling with Submodular Guarantees
authors: Xiaoyu Ma, Yiwen Li, Haoyue Liu, Zhichao Wang, Ye Chen, Yongxin Guo, Xiaoying Tang
abstract: Automatic prompt optimization (APO) hinges on the quality of its evaluation signal, yet scoring every prompt candidate on the full training set is prohibitively expensive. Existing methods either fix a single evaluation subset before optimization begins (principled but prompt-agnostic) or adapt it heuristically during optimization (flexible but unstable and lacking formal guarantees). We observe that APO naturally maps to an online adaptive testing problem: prompts are examinees, training examples are test items, and the scheduler should select items that best discriminate among the strongest candidates. This insight motivates Prompt-Aware Online Evaluation Scheduling (POES), which integrates an IRT-based discrimination utility, a facility-location coverage term, and switching-cost-aware warm-start swaps into a unified objective that is provably monotone submodular, yielding a (1-1/e) greedy guarantee for cold starts and bounded drift for warm-start updates. An adaptive controller modulates the exploration-exploitation balance based on optimization progress. Across 36 tasks spanning three benchmark families, POES achieves the highest overall average accuracy (6.2 percent improvement over the best baseline) with negligible token overhead (approximately 4 percent) at the same evaluation budget. Moreover, principled selection at k = 20 examples matches or exceeds the performance of naive evaluation at k = 30-50, reducing token consumption by 35-60 percent, showing that selecting smarter is more effective than selecting more. Our results demonstrate that evaluation scheduling is a first-class component of APO, not an implementation detail.
title_zh: 选得更聪明，而非更多：具有子模保证的提示感知评估调度
abstract_zh: 自动提示优化（APO）的核心在于其评估信号的质量，然而在完整的训练集上对每个提示候选进行评分的开销极为高昂。现有方法要么在优化开始前固定一个单一的评估子集（虽具理论严谨性但与提示无关），要么在优化过程中启发式地动态调整评估子集（虽灵活但不稳定且缺乏形式化保证）。我们观察到，APO天然地可建模为一个在线自适应测试问题：提示相当于考生，训练样本相当于测试题目，调度器应选择最能区分最强候选提示的题目。这一洞察催生了**提示感知的在线评估调度**（Prompt-Aware Online Evaluation Scheduling, POES），该方法将基于项目反应理论（IRT）的区分度效用、设施位置覆盖项以及考虑切换成本的热启动替换机制整合进一个统一的目标函数，该目标函数被证明具有单调子模性，从而在冷启动情况下提供(1−1/e)的贪心近似保证，并在热启动更新中实现有界漂移。一个自适应控制器根据优化进展动态调节探索与利用之间的平衡。在涵盖三个基准数据集家族共36个任务上的实验表明，POES在相同评估预算下实现了最高的平均准确率（相比最佳基线提升6.2%），且额外的token开销极低（约4%）。此外，在仅选取k=20个样例时，经过精心设计的选择性能已达到甚至超过盲目选取k=30–50个样例的效果，使token消耗降低35%至60%，充分证明“选得更聪明”比“选得更多”更为有效。我们的研究结果表明，评估调度是APO的一个核心组成部分，而非单纯的实现细节。

title: Evaluating LLM Agents on Automated Software Analysis Tasks
authors: Michael Pradel, Cristian Cadar, Islem Bouzenia
abstract: Numerous software analysis tools exist today, yet applying them to diverse open-source projects remains challenging due to environment setup, dependency resolution, and tool configuration. LLM-based agents offer a potential solution, yet no prior work has systematically studied their effectiveness on the specific task of automated software analysis, which, unlike issue solving or general environment setup, requires installing and configuring a separate analysis tool alongside the target project, generating tool-specific prerequisites, and validating that the tool produces meaningful analysis outputs. We introduce AnalysisBench, a benchmark of 35 tool-project pairs spanning seven analysis tools and ten diverse C/C++ and Java projects, each with a manually constructed reference setup. Using AnalysisBench, we evaluate four agent architectures across four LLM backends. Our custom agent, AnalysisAgent, achieves manually verified success rates of 94% (Gemini-3-Flash, 33/35 tasks), compared to 77% for the best baseline (ExecutionAgent). Beyond quantitative results, we identify key limitations in existing agents, including stage mixing, poor error localization, and premature termination, and show that agentic architecture matters more than LLM capability alone. We further find that whole-program analyses and symbolic execution are the most difficult tasks, that Java toolchains pose greater challenges than C/C++, and that LLM-self-validated success consistently overstates manually verified success.
title_zh: 评估大型语言模型代理在自动化软件分析任务中的表现
abstract_zh: 如今存在众多软件分析工具，但将这些工具应用于不同的开源项目仍面临诸多挑战，包括环境配置、依赖项解析以及工具设置等问题。基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体（agent）为解决这些问题提供了潜在方案，然而此前尚无研究系统地探讨其在自动化软件分析任务中的有效性。与问题求解或通用环境搭建不同，自动化软件分析不仅需要在目标项目中安装和配置独立的分析工具，还需生成特定于工具的前置条件，并验证工具是否产生有意义的分析结果。为此，我们提出了AnalysisBench——一个包含35个“工具-项目”组合的基准测试集，覆盖七种分析工具和十个多样化的C/C++及Java项目，每个组合均配有手动构建的参考配置。利用AnalysisBench，我们在四种LLM后端上评估了四种代理架构。我们自研的AnalysisAgent在Gemini-3-Flash支持下取得了94%的手动验证成功率（33/35个任务），显著优于表现最佳的基线方法ExecutionAgent（77%）。除了量化结果外，我们还识别出现有代理存在的若干关键局限：阶段混淆、错误定位能力差以及过早终止等问题，并表明代理架构的设计比LLM本身的能力更为重要。此外，我们发现全程序分析和符号执行是最具挑战性的任务类型；Java工具链相比C/C++更具挑战性；且LLM自我验证的成功率往往高估了实际的人工验证结果。

title: Judge Like Human Examiners: A Weighted Importance Multi-Point Evaluation Framework for Generative Tasks with Long-form Answers
authors: Guoxin Yu, Chulun Zhou, Lemao Liu, Qi Wang, Mo Yu, Jialong Tang, Baosong Yang, Xiang Ao, Wao Lam, Yue Yu
abstract: Evaluating the quality of model responses remains challenging in generative tasks with long-form answers, as the expected answers usually contain multiple semantically distinct yet complementary factors that should be factorized for fine-grained assessment. Recent evaluation methods resort to relying on either task-level rubrics or question-aware checklists. However, they still 1) struggle to assess whether a response is genuinely grounded in provided contexts; 2) fail to capture the heterogeneous importance of different aspects of reference answers. Inspired by human examiners, we propose a Weighted Importance Multi-Point Evaluation (WIMPE) framework, which factorizes each reference answer into weighted context-bound scoring points. Two complementary metrics, namely Weighted Point-wise Alignment (WPA) and Point-wise Conflict Penalty (PCP), are designed to measure the alignment and contradiction between model responses and reference answers. Extensive experiments on 10 generative tasks demonstrate that WIMPE achieves higher correlations with human annotations.
title_zh: 像人类评审员一样进行评判：一种用于生成任务中长篇回答的加权重要性多点评估框架
abstract_zh: 在生成任务中，尤其是需要长篇回答的任务中，评估模型输出的质量仍然具有挑战性。这是因为预期答案通常包含多个语义上不同但相互补充的要素，这些要素需要被分解以实现细粒度的评估。现有的评估方法主要依赖于任务层面的评分标准或与问题相关的检查清单，但仍存在两个关键缺陷：1）难以判断模型的回答是否真正基于所提供的上下文；2）无法捕捉参考答案中不同方面的重要性差异。受人类阅卷者的启发，我们提出了加权重要性多点评估（Weighted Importance Multi-Point Evaluation, WIMPE）框架，该框架将每个参考答案分解为一系列加权的、与上下文相关的评分点。为此，我们设计了两种互补的度量指标：加权逐点对齐度（Weighted Point-wise Alignment, WPA）和逐点冲突惩罚（Point-wise Conflict Penalty, PCP），分别用于衡量模型回答与参考答案之间的对齐程度和矛盾情况。在10个生成任务上的大量实验表明，WIMPE与人工标注的相关性更高，展现出更优的评估性能。

title: Hardware-Efficient Compound IC Protection with Lightweight Cryptography
authors: Levent Aksoy, Muhammad Sohaib Munir, Sedat Akleylek
abstract: Over the years, many techniques have been introduced to protect integrated circuits (ICs) from hardware security threats that emerged in the globalized IC manufacturing supply chain, such as overproduction and piracy. However, most of these techniques have been rendered inefficient since they do not rely on provably secure algorithms. Moreover, the previously proposed techniques using cryptography algorithms lead to a significant increase in hardware complexity and are vulnerable to the removal and power analysis attacks. In this paper, we propose a compound IC protection mechanism that uses a lightweight cryptography algorithm with prominent logic locking and hardware obfuscation techniques. Experimental results show that the secure designs generated by the developed tool have significantly less hardware complexity when compared to those generated by previously proposed techniques using cryptography algorithms and are resilient to existing removal, algebraic, and logic locking attacks.
title_zh: 基于轻量级密码学的高效硬件复合集成电路防护
abstract_zh: 多年来，为应对全球化集成电路（IC）制造供应链中出现的硬件安全威胁（如过度生产与盗版），已引入多种保护技术。然而，由于这些技术大多依赖于无法严格证明安全性的算法，因此其有效性已大打折扣。此外，以往采用密码学算法的保护方法不仅导致硬件复杂度显著增加，还容易受到移除攻击和功耗分析攻击的威胁。本文提出了一种复合型集成电路保护机制，该机制结合了轻量级密码算法以及突出的逻辑锁定与硬件混淆技术。实验结果表明，由所开发工具生成的安全设计在硬件复杂度方面显著低于以往基于密码学算法的技术，并且对现有的移除攻击、代数攻击及逻辑锁定攻击均具有较强的抗性。

title: DeCoVec: Building Decoding Space based Task Vector for Large Language Models via In-Context Learning
authors: Feiyang Li, Yile Wang
abstract: Task vectors, representing directions in model or activation spaces that encode task-specific behaviors, have emerged as a promising tool for steering large language models (LLMs). However, existing approaches typically require fine-tuning or invasive manipulation of internal states, limiting their flexibility and scalability. We propose \textsc{DeCoVec} (Decoding Space based Task Vector), a training-free and non-invasive framework that constructs task vectors directly in the \textit{decoding space} by leveraging in-context learning (ICL). Specifically, \textsc{DeCoVec} captures the task essence as the difference between the output logit distributions of few-shot and zero-shot prompts, then steers generation by injecting this vector into the decoding process. Experiments across seven LLMs (0.5B--9B) on TruthfulQA, Math-500, and AQUA-RAT show that \textsc{DeCoVec} consistently outperforms standard few-shot baselines, with gains up to +5.50 average accuracy. Further analysis demonstrates that \textsc{DeCoVec} effectively suppresses generation degeneration and logical flaws while exhibiting strong robustness to demonstration ordering, all without incurring additional input token costs. Our method offers a training-free and non-invasive solution for LLM steering without requiring weight updates or auxiliary models.
title_zh: DeCoVec：基于上下文学习构建解码空间任务向量以用于大型语言模型
abstract_zh: 任务向量（task vectors）代表了模型或激活空间中的方向，用于编码特定任务的行为，已成为引导大型语言模型（LLMs）的一种有前景的工具。然而，现有方法通常需要微调或对内部状态进行侵入式操作，限制了其灵活性和可扩展性。我们提出 \textsc{DeCoVec}（基于解码空间的任务向量），一种无需训练且非侵入性的框架，通过利用上下文学习（ICL）直接在 \textit{解码空间} 中构建任务向量。具体而言，\textsc{DeCoVec} 将任务本质捕捉为少样本提示与零样本提示输出logit分布之间的差异，并通过将该向量注入解码过程来引导生成。在 TruthfulQA、Math-500 和 AQUA-RAT 上对七种 LLM（规模从 0.5B 到 9B）的实验表明，\textsc{DeCoVec} 始终优于标准的少样本基线，平均准确率提升最高达 +5.50。进一步分析显示，\textsc{DeCoVec} 能有效抑制生成退化和逻辑错误，同时对示范顺序具有强鲁棒性，且不增加额外的输入 token 成本。我们的方法提供了一种无需训练、非侵入式的 LLM 引导方案，无需权重更新或辅助模型。

title: Record-Remix-Replay: Hierarchical GPU Kernel Optimization using Evolutionary Search
authors: Daniel Nichols, Konstantinos Parasyris, Caetano Melone, Tal Ben-Nun, Giorgis Georgakoudis, Harshitha Menon
abstract: As high-performance computing and AI workloads become increasingly dependent on GPUs, maintaining high performance across rapidly evolving hardware generations has become a major challenge. Developers often spend months tuning scientific applications to fully exploit new architectures, navigating a complex optimization space that spans algorithm design, source implementation, compiler flags and pass sequences, and kernel launch parameters. Existing approaches can effectively search parts of this space in isolation, such as launch configurations or compiler settings, but optimizing across the full space still requires substantial human expertise and iterative manual effort. In this paper, we present Record-Remix-Replay (R^3), a hierarchical optimization framework that combines LLM-driven evolutionary search, Bayesian optimization, and record-replay compilation techniques to efficiently explore GPU kernel optimizations from source-level implementation choices down to compiler pass ordering and runtime configuration. By making candidate evaluation fast and scalable, our approach enables practical end-to-end search over optimization dimensions that are typically treated separately. We show that Record-Remix-Replay can optimize full scientific applications better than traditional approaches over kernel parameters and compiler flags, while also being nearly an order of magnitude faster than modern evolutionary search approaches.
title_zh: 记录-重混-重播：基于进化搜索的分层GPU内核优化
abstract_zh: 随着高性能计算和人工智能工作负载对GPU的依赖日益加深，如何在快速演进的硬件架构中保持高性能已成为一大挑战。开发者往往需要花费数月时间对科学计算应用进行调优，以充分挖掘新型架构的潜力，而这一过程涉及复杂的优化空间，涵盖算法设计、源码实现、编译器选项与优化阶段序列，以及内核启动参数等多个方面。现有的方法虽然能在某些独立维度（如运行时配置或编译器设置）上有效搜索，但要对整个优化空间进行全面优化，仍需大量人工经验与反复的手动迭代。本文提出了一种分层优化框架——Record-Remix-Replay（R³），该框架融合了基于大语言模型的进化搜索、贝叶斯优化以及记录-重播编译技术，能够从源码级实现选择，到编译器优化阶段顺序，再到运行时配置，高效探索GPU内核优化的全维度空间。通过实现快速且可扩展的候选方案评估，我们的方法使得原本通常被割裂处理的优化维度得以实现实际可行的端到端联合搜索。实验表明，R³在优化完整科学应用方面，相比传统方法在内核参数与编译器标志上的表现更优，同时其速度也比现代的进化搜索方法快近一个数量级。

title: AnomalyGen: Enhancing Log-Based Anomaly Detection with Code-Guided Data Augmentation
authors: Xinyu Li, Yintong Huo, Chenxi Mao, Shiwen Shan, Yuxin Su, Yanlin Wang, Zibin Zheng
abstract: Log-based anomaly detection is fundamentally constrained by training data sparsity. Our empirical study reveals that public benchmark datasets cover less than 10% of source code log templates. Consequently, models frequently misclassify unseen but valid execution paths as anomalies, leading to false alarms. To address this, we propose AnomalyGen, a novel framework that augments training data by synthesizing labeled log sequences from source code. AnomalyGen combines log-oriented static analysis with Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning in three stages: (1) building Log-Oriented Control Flow Graphs (LCFGs) to enumerate structurally valid execution paths; (2) applying LLM Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning to verify logical consistency and generate realistic runtime parameters (e.g., block IDs, IP addresses); and (3) labeling generated sequences with domain heuristics. Evaluations on HDFS and Zookeeper across 12 diverse anomaly detection models show AnomalyGen consistently improves performance. Deep learning models achieved average F1-score gains of 2.18% (HDFS) and 1.69% (Zookeeper), with an unsupervised Transformer on HDFS jumping from 0.818 to 0.970. Ablation results show that both static analysis and LLM-based verification are necessary: removing them reduces F1 by up to 8.7 and 10.7 percentage points, respectively. Our framework and datasets are publicly available to facilitate future research.
title_zh: AnomalyGen：基于代码引导的数据增强提升日志异常检测
abstract_zh: 基于日志的异常检测在本质上受限于训练数据的稀疏性。我们的实证研究发现，公开基准数据集仅覆盖了不到10%的源代码日志模板。因此，模型经常将未见过但实际有效的执行路径误判为异常，导致大量误报。为解决这一问题，我们提出AnomalyGen——一种新颖的框架，通过从源代码中合成带标签的日志序列来扩充训练数据。AnomalyGen在三个阶段结合面向日志的静态分析与大语言模型（LLM）推理：(1) 构建面向日志的控制流图（LCFG），以枚举结构上有效的执行路径；(2) 利用LLM的思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）推理验证逻辑一致性，并生成真实的运行时参数（如块ID、IP地址等）；(3) 使用领域启发式规则对生成的日志序列进行标注。在HDFS和Zookeeper上对12种不同的异常检测模型进行评估的结果表明，AnomalyGen能持续提升性能。深度学习模型在HDFS上的F1分数平均提升2.18%，在Zookeeper上提升1.69%；其中，无监督Transformer模型在HDFS上的F1分数从0.818跃升至0.970。消融实验显示，静态分析与基于LLM的验证均不可或缺：移除任一模块会使F1分数分别下降最多达8.7和10.7个百分点。我们的框架及数据集已公开发布，以促进未来的研究工作。

title: From Context to Rules: Toward Unified Detection Rule Generation
authors: Cheng Meng, Wenxin Le, Xinyi Li, Qiuyun Wang, Fangli Ren, Zhengwei Jiang, Baoxu Liu
abstract: Existing methods for detection rule generation are tightly coupled to specific input-output combinations, requiring dedicated pipelines for each. We formalize this problem as a unified mapping f:C*L->R and characterize optimal rules through semantic distance. We propose UniRule, an agentic RAG framework built on dual semantic projection spaces: detection intent and detection logic. This design enables retrieval and generation across arbitrary contexts and target languages within a single system. Experiments across 12 scenarios (3 languages, 4 context types, 12,000 pairwise comparisons) show that UniRule significantly outperforms pure LLM generation with a Bradley-Terry coefficient of 0.52, validating semantic projection as an effective abstraction for unified rule generation. Together, the formalization, method, and evaluation provide an initial framework for studying detection rule generation as a unified task.
title_zh: 从上下文到规则：迈向统一的检测规则生成
abstract_zh: 现有检测规则生成方法与特定的输入-输出组合紧密耦合，需为每种组合单独设计处理流程。我们将其形式化为统一映射 f:C×L→R，并通过语义距离来刻画最优规则。为此，我们提出 UniRule——一种基于双重语义投影空间（检测意图与检测逻辑）的代理式 RAG 框架。该设计使系统能够在单一架构内实现跨任意上下文和目标语言的检索与生成。在12个场景（3种语言、4类上下文、共12,000对比较）中的实验表明，UniRule 显著优于纯大模型生成方法，其 Bradley-Terry 系数达 0.52，验证了语义投影作为统一规则生成有效抽象的可行性。综上，形式化定义、方法设计与评估体系共同构建了一个初步框架，为将检测规则生成视为统一任务的研究提供了基础。

title: Sema Code: Decoupling AI Coding Agents into Programmable, Embeddable Infrastructure
authors: Huacan Wang, Jie Zhou, Ningyan Zhu, Shuo Zhang, Feiyu Chen, Jiarou Wu, Ge Chen, Chen Liu, Wangyi Chen, Xiaofeng Mou, Yi Xu
abstract: AI coding agents have become central to developer workflows, yet every existing solution locks its reasoning capabilities within a specific delivery form, such as a CLI, IDE plugin, or web application. This limitation creates systemic barriers when enterprises attempt to reuse these capabilities across heterogeneous engineering environments. To address this challenge, we present Sema Code, an open AI coding framework built on the principle of being embeddable, pluggable, and framework-first. Sema Code completely decouples the core agent engine from all client layers, publishing it as a standalone npm library that any runtime can drive programmatically. Built around this architecture, we designed eight key mechanisms: multi-tenant engine isolation, FIFO input queuing with safe session reconstruction, adaptive context compression, multi-agent collaborative scheduling, intelligent Todo-based process management, four-layer asynchronous permission control, three-tier ecosystem integration spanning MCP, Skills, and Plugins, and a background task framework with separated execution and observation privileges. These mechanisms collectively address the engineering challenges of transforming a complex agent engine into a shared, programmable core. Demonstrating its architectural versatility, the same Sema Core engine simultaneously powers a VSCode extension and a multi-channel messaging gateway, which we name SemaClaw, to unify agent interactions across platforms such as Telegram and Feishu. These represent two fundamentally different product forms sharing an identical reasoning kernel, differing only at the client layer.
title_zh: Sema代码：将AI编码代理解耦为可编程、可嵌入的基础设施
abstract_zh:
AI 编码代理已逐渐成为开发者工作流的核心，然而现有所有解决方案都将推理能力锁定在特定的交付形式中，例如命令行界面（CLI）、IDE 插件或 Web 应用。这种局限性在企业试图将这些能力跨异构工程环境复用时，造成了系统性的障碍。为应对这一挑战，我们提出 Sema Code——一个基于可嵌入、可插拔、框架优先原则构建的开源 AI 编码框架。Sema Code 完全解耦核心代理引擎与所有客户端层，将其发布为一个独立的 npm 库，任何运行时均可通过编程方式驱动。

围绕这一架构，我们设计了八大关键机制：多租户引擎隔离、带安全会话重建的 FIFO 输入队列、自适应上下文压缩、多代理协同调度、基于智能 Todo 的流程管理、四层异步权限控制、涵盖 MCP、技能（Skills）和插件三个层级的三层生态系统集成，以及具备执行与观察权限分离的后台任务框架。这些机制共同解决了将复杂代理引擎转化为共享、可编程核心所面临的工程挑战。

为展示其架构的灵活性，相同的 Sema Core 引擎同时驱动了一个 VSCode 扩展和一个多通道消息网关，我们称之为 SemaClaw，用于统一 Telegram、飞书等平台上的代理交互。这两种产品形态本质完全不同，却共享同一套推理内核，仅在客户端层存在差异。

title: Sanity Checks for Agentic Data Science
authors: Zachary T. Rewolinski, Austin V. Zane, Hao Huang, Chandan Singh, Chenglong Wang, Jianfeng Gao, Bin Yu
abstract: Agentic data science (ADS) pipelines have grown rapidly in both capability and adoption, with systems such as OpenAI Codex now able to directly analyze datasets and produce answers to statistical questions. However, these systems can reach falsely optimistic conclusions that are difficult for users to detect. To address this, we propose a pair of lightweight sanity checks grounded in the Predictability-Computability-Stability (PCS) framework for veridical data science. These checks use reasonable perturbations to screen whether an agent can reliably distinguish signal from noise, acting as a falsifiability constraint that can expose affirmative conclusions as unsupported. Together, the two checks characterize the trustworthiness of an ADS output, e.g. whether it has found stable signal, is responding to noise, or is sensitive to incidental aspects of the input. We validate the approach on synthetic data with controlled signal-to-noise ratios, confirming that the sanity checks track ground-truth signal strength. We then demonstrate the checks on 11 real-world datasets using OpenAI Codex, characterizing the trustworthiness of each conclusion and finding that in 6 of the datasets an affirmative conclusion is not well-supported, even though a single ADS run may support one. We further analyze failure modes of ADS systems and find that ADS self-reported confidence is poorly calibrated to the empirical stability of its conclusions.
title_zh: 代理型数据科学的合理性检查
abstract_zh: 代理型数据科学（Agentic Data Science, ADS）管道在能力与应用方面均迅速发展，如今像 OpenAI Codex 这样的系统已能直接分析数据集，并对统计问题生成答案。然而，这些系统可能得出看似合理实则错误乐观的结论，而用户往往难以察觉。为应对这一挑战，我们提出了一套基于“可预测性-可计算性-稳定性”（Predictability-Computability-Stability, PCS）框架的轻量级合理性检验方法，以实现真实可信的数据科学。这两项检验通过施加合理的数据扰动，评估代理是否能够可靠地区分信号与噪声，从而作为可证伪性的约束机制，揭示出某些肯定性结论缺乏充分支持。综合来看，这两个检验共同刻画了ADS输出的可信度，例如判断其是否发现了稳定的信号、是否响应了噪声，或是否对输入中的偶然因素过于敏感。我们在具有可控信噪比的合成数据上验证了该方法，结果表明这些合理性检验能够准确追踪真实的信号强度。随后，我们利用 OpenAI Codex 在11个真实世界数据集上应用该检验方法，评估各结论的可信度，发现其中6个数据集的肯定性结论并未得到充分支持，尽管单次ADS运行可能看似支持该结论。此外，我们进一步分析了ADS系统的失效模式，发现其自我报告的信心水平与结论的实证稳定性之间存在严重脱节。

title: Visible, Trackable, Forkable: Opening the Process of Science
authors: Sergey V. Samsonau
abstract: The way science is currently practiced shows conclusions but hides how they were reached. Researchers work privately, polish their results, publish a finished paper, and defend it. Errors are punished by retraction rather than corrected by amendment. Alternative directions are pursued through competing papers with no shared history. The reasoning, the dead ends, the trade-offs, the corrections: everything that would let others understand how a conclusion was reached is invisible. Two decades of open science reform have addressed this by opening specific artifacts: papers, data, code, notebooks, protocols. Each is valuable, but the unit remains a finished product. None opens the thinking process itself: the evolving sequence of questions, interpretations, dead ends, and direction changes that constitutes the actual scientific contribution. This paper argues that opening the process of science (not just its outputs) would produce a step change in the speed of scientific progress, the accessibility of scientific reasoning, the trustworthiness of scientific claims, and the scalability of scientific quality assurance. We identify three properties the workflow needs: visible (the process is open, not just the product), trackable (every change is recorded and attributable), and forkable (anyone can branch from any point with shared history preserved). A visible, trackable flow is inherently verifiable: by humans, by automated tools, by AI agents. Software development adopted this flow decades ago, and the results (faster correction, broader contribution, maintained quality at scale) demonstrate the opportunity for science.
title_zh: 可见、可追踪、可复现：科学过程的开放之道
abstract_zh:
当前科学实践的方式展示的是结论，却隐藏了得出这些结论的过程。研究人员在私下工作，精心打磨结果，发布一篇完整的论文并加以辩护。一旦出现错误，通常不是通过修正来纠正，而是以撤稿作为惩罚。不同的研究方向则通过彼此独立的论文展开，彼此之间没有共同的历史记录。推理过程、走过的弯路、权衡取舍以及修正调整——所有这些本能让他人理解结论如何形成的内容，都变得不可见。过去二十年的开放科学改革虽然已有所进展，通过开放特定成果如论文、数据、代码、实验笔记和操作规程等，但这些努力仍局限于“成品”的开放。它们并未揭示科学思维本身的过程：即问题演变、解释变化、走投无路的尝试以及方向调整的动态序列——而这正是科学贡献的真实所在。

本文主张，若能开放科学研究的过程（而不仅仅是其产出），将带来科学进步速度、科学推理可理解性、科学主张可信度以及科学质量保障可扩展性的根本性提升。我们提出，这一工作流程必须具备三个核心属性：**可见性**（过程公开，而不仅是成果）、**可追踪性**（每一次变更都有记录且可追溯）和**可分叉性**（任何人都可以从任意节点分叉，同时保留共享的历史）。一个具有可见性和可追踪性的流程，本质上是可验证的——无论是由人类、自动化工具还是AI代理进行验证。软件开发领域几十年前就已采用这种流程，其成果（更快地发现并修复错误、更广泛的协作参与、在规模化下依然保持高质量）充分证明了科学界同样拥有巨大的潜力。

title: Ambiguity Detection and Elimination in Automated Executable Process Modeling
authors: Ion Matei, Praveen Kumar Menaka Sekar, Maksym Zhenirovskyy, Hon Yung Wong, Sayuri Kohmura, Shinji Hotta, Akihiro Inomata
abstract: Automated generation of executable Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) models from natural-language specifications is increasingly enabled by large language models. However, ambiguous or underspecified text can yield structurally valid models with different simulated behavior. Our goal is not to prove that one generated BPMN model is semantically correct, but to detect when a natural-language specification fails to support a stable executable interpretation under repeated generation and simulation. We present a diagnosis-driven framework that detects behavioral inconsistency from the empirical distribution of key performance indicators (KPIs), localizes divergence to gateway logic using model-based diagnosis, maps that logic back to verbatim narrative segments, and repairs the source text through evidence-based refinement. Experiments on diabetic nephropathy health-guidance policies show that the method reduces variability in regenerated model behavior. The result is a closed-loop approach for validating and repairing executable process specifications in the absence of ground-truth BPMN models.
title_zh: 自动化可执行流程建模中的歧义检测与消除
abstract_zh: 由大型语言模型支持，从自然语言规范自动生成可执行的业务流程建模与标注（BPMN）模型正变得日益普遍。然而，模糊或描述不充分的文本可能导致生成结构上有效但行为各异的BPMN模型。我们的目标并非证明某个生成的BPMN模型在语义上完全正确，而是检测当自然语言规范无法在多次生成和仿真中支持稳定可执行解释时的情况。本文提出一种诊断驱动的框架：通过关键绩效指标（KPI）的经验分布检测行为不一致性，利用基于模型的诊断方法定位到网关逻辑中的差异根源，并将这些逻辑映射回原始叙述段落，最终通过基于证据的文本优化实现源文本修复。在糖尿病肾病健康指导政策的实验中，该方法显著降低了再生模型行为的变异性。结果形成了一种闭环方法，可在缺乏真实BPMN模型作为基准的情况下，对可执行流程规范进行验证与修复。

title: FM-Agent: Scaling Formal Methods to Large Systems via LLM-Based Hoare-Style Reasoning
authors: Haoran Ding, Zhaoguo Wang, Haibo Chen
abstract: LLM-assisted software development has become increasingly prevalent, and can generate large-scale systems, such as compilers. It becomes crucial to strengthen the correctness of the generated code. However, automated reasoning for large-scale systems remains challenging due to code complexity. Hoare logic offers an approach to decomposing a large system into smaller components and reasoning about them separately (i.e., compositional reasoning). However, existing works still struggle to scale, because Hoare logic requires writing formal specifications for each function, imposing a heavy human burden. The problem is exacerbated when code is generated by LLMs, as developers lack a deep understanding of each function's expected behavior. This paper presents FM-Agent, the first framework that realizes automated compositional reasoning for large-scale systems. Leveraging LLMs, FM-Agent introduces a top-down paradigm to automatically generate function-level specifications. Specifically, FM-Agent derives the specification of a function from how its callers expect the function to behave, so the generated specifications can reflect the developer's intent of a function even if the implementation is buggy. Developers' intent is usually expressed in natural language, while existing verifiers only support formulas. Therefore, FM-Agent generalizes Hoare-style inference to reason about functions against natural-language specifications. Finally, to confirm bug existence and explain bug causes, FM-Agent automatically generates test cases to trigger potential bugs. In our evaluation, FM-Agent successfully reasons about large-scale systems within 2 days, each of which has up to 143k LoC. These systems have already been tested by their developers, but FM-Agent still finds 522 newly discovered bugs. These bugs can cause serious consequences, including system crashes and incorrect execution results.
title_zh: FM-Agent：基于大语言模型的霍雷风格推理，实现形式化方法在大型系统中的扩展
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的软件开发日益普及，能够生成大规模系统，如编译器。因此，增强生成代码的正确性变得至关重要。然而，由于代码本身的复杂性，对大规模系统的自动化推理仍然面临挑战。霍尔逻辑（Hoare Logic）提供了一种将大型系统分解为较小组件并分别进行推理的方法（即组合式推理）。但现有方法在扩展性上仍存在困难，因为霍尔逻辑要求为每个函数编写形式化规格说明，这给开发者带来了沉重的人工负担。当代码由LLM生成时，这一问题更加严重，因为开发者往往无法深入理解每个函数预期的行为。本文提出了FM-Agent，这是首个实现大规模系统自动化组合式推理的框架。FM-Agent利用LLM，引入自顶向下的范式，自动为函数生成层级规格说明。具体而言，FM-Agent通过分析调用者对函数行为的期望来推导出该函数的规格说明，使得生成的规格即使在实现存在缺陷的情况下，也能反映开发者对该函数的意图。通常，开发者的意图以自然语言表达，而现有的验证工具仅支持公式化表达。为此，FM-Agent将霍尔风格的推理推广至支持自然语言规格说明的推理。最后，为了确认缺陷的存在并解释其成因，FM-Agent会自动生成测试用例以触发潜在的缺陷。在我们的评估中，FM-Agent在两天内成功完成了对多个大规模系统的推理，每个系统代码量高达14.3万行。这些系统此前已由开发者完成测试，但FM-Agent仍发现了522个全新的漏洞。这些漏洞可能导致严重后果，包括系统崩溃和执行结果错误。

title: Low-rank Optimization Trajectories Modeling for LLM RLVR Acceleration
authors: Zhipeng Chen, Tao Qian, Wayne Xin Zhao, Ji-Rong Wen
abstract: Recently, scaling reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) for large language models (LLMs) has emerged as an effective training paradigm for significantly improving model capabilities, which requires guiding the model to perform extensive exploration and learning, leading to substantial computational overhead and becoming a key challenge. To reduce the number of training steps, Prior work performs linear extrapolation of model parameters. However, the dynamics of model parameter updates during RLVR training remain insufficiently understood. To further investigate the evolution of LLMs during RLVR training, we conduct empirical experiments and find that the rank-1 subspace of the model does not evolve linearly, and its dominance over the original parameters is further amplified during LoRA training. Based on the above insights, we propose the \textbf{N}onlinear \textbf{Ext}rapolation of low-rank trajectories (\textbf{NExt}), a novel framework that models and extrapolates low-rank parameter trajectories in a nonlinear manner. Concretely, we first train the model using LoRA and extract the rank-1 subspace of parameter differences at multiple training steps, which is then used for the subsequent nonlinear extrapolation. Afterward, we utilized the extracted rank-1 subspace to train a predictor, which can model the trajectory of parameter updates during RLVR, and then perform the predict-extend process to extrapolate model parameters, achieving the acceleration of RLVR. To further study and understand NExt, we conduct comprehensive experiments that demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the method. Our method reduces computational overhead by approximately 37.5\% while remaining compatible with a wide range of RLVR algorithms and tasks. We release our code in https://github.com/RUCAIBox/NExt.
title_zh: 用于大语言模型强化学习视觉推理加速的低秩优化轨迹建模
abstract_zh: 最近，基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）在大规模语言模型（LLMs）中的应用已成为一种有效提升模型能力的训练范式。该方法需要引导模型进行广泛探索与学习，从而带来巨大的计算开销，成为当前面临的关键挑战。为减少训练步数，已有研究采用模型参数的线性外推方法。然而，对RLVR训练过程中模型参数更新动态的理解仍不充分。为进一步探究LLM在RLVR训练过程中的演化规律，我们开展了实证实验，发现模型的秩-1子空间并非线性演化，且在LoRA训练过程中其对原始参数的影响进一步被放大。基于上述发现，我们提出了**NExt**（Nonlinear Extrapolation of low-rank trajectories）——一种新型框架，通过非线性方式建模并外推低秩参数轨迹。具体而言，我们首先使用LoRA训练模型，并在多个训练阶段提取参数差异的秩-1子空间，随后利用该子空间构建预测器，以建模RLVR过程中参数更新的轨迹，并通过“预测-扩展”流程实现模型参数的外推，从而加速RLVR训练。为深入研究和理解NExt，我们进行了全面的实验，结果表明该方法具有优异的有效性与鲁棒性：在保持与多种RLVR算法及任务高度兼容的前提下，可将计算开销降低约37.5%。相关代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/RUCAIBox/NExt。

title: From GDSII to Wafer: EDA Design Flow and Data Conversion for Wafer-Scale Manufacturing of Superconducting Quantum Chips
authors: Ling Qiao, Fumin Luo, Qinglang Guo
abstract: Superconducting quantum computing is advancing toward the thousand- and even million-qubit regime, making wafer-scale fabrication an essential pathway for achieving large-scale, cost-effective quantum processors. This manufacturing paradigm imposes new requirements on quantum-chip electronic design automation (Q-EDA): design tools must not only generate layouts (GDSII files) that satisfy quantum-circuit physical constraints but also ensure that the design data can be seamlessly converted into a complete set of manufacturing files executable by a wafer foundry, thereby enabling reliable translation from design intent to physical chip. This paper focuses on this critical data-conversion pipeline and presents a systematic treatment of the Q-EDA technology stack for wafer-scale fabrication. Starting from GDSII as the single authoritative data source, we analyze the key stages including process-design-kit (PDK)-based design rule checking (DRC), layout-versus-schematic (LVS) verification, design for manufacturability (DFM) optimization, wafer layout planning, and mask data preparation (MDP). We describe the concrete architecture of a Q-EDA system, present nine quantum-specific DRC rules together with their physical underpinnings and a multi-layer process stack model, and benchmark the manufacturing data-flow coverage of mainstream Q-EDA tools. Finally, we discuss the core challenges and future directions in this field.
title_zh: 从GDSII到晶圆：超导量子芯片晶圆级制造的EDA设计流程与数据转换
abstract_zh: 超导量子计算正朝着千比特乃至百万比特的规模迈进，这使得晶圆级制造成为实现大规模、低成本量子处理器的关键路径。这一制造范式对量子芯片电子设计自动化（Q-EDA）提出了新的要求：设计工具不仅需要生成满足量子电路物理约束的版图（GDSII文件），还必须确保设计数据能够无缝转换为晶圆代工厂可执行的完整制造文件，从而可靠地实现从设计意图到物理芯片的转化。本文聚焦于这一关键的数据转换流程，系统性地阐述了面向晶圆级制造的Q-EDA技术栈。以GDSII作为唯一权威数据源，我们分析了包括基于工艺设计套件（PDK）的设计规则检查（DRC）、版图与原理图对比（LVS）验证、可制造性设计（DFM）优化、晶圆版图规划以及掩模数据准备（MDP）在内的关键环节。文中描述了一个Q-EDA系统的具体架构，提出了九条针对量子器件的特定DRC规则，并阐明其物理基础，同时构建了多层工艺堆叠模型；此外，还对主流Q-EDA工具的制造数据流覆盖能力进行了基准测试。最后，本文探讨了该领域面临的核心挑战及未来发展方向。

title: CLSGen: A Dual-Head Fine-Tuning Framework for Joint Probabilistic Classification and Verbalized Explanation
authors: WonJin Yoon, Kangyu Zhu, Ian Bulovic, Autumn Sehy, Yanjun Gao, Dmitriy Dligach, Majid Afshar, Timothy A. Miller
abstract: With the recent progress of Large Language Models (LLMs), there is a growing interest in applying these models to solve complex and challenging problems. Modern LLMs, capable of processing long contexts and generating verbalized explanations, offer significant potential in addressing real-world applications. However, a critical hurdle in deploying LLMs for practical decision-making is their inability to provide reliable, quantitative probabilities. While task-specific fine-tuning of LLMs using traditional discriminative objectives (similar to encoder-only models) can yield probability estimates, this often leads to catastrophic forgetting and linguistic collapse. Consequently, the model loses its ability to generate explanations, severely undermining its interpretability and usability. To address this challenge, we propose CLSGen, a novel LLM fine-tuning framework designed for binary classification tasks. The CLSGen framework encompasses a new model architecture, training methodology, and data construction strategy to enable robust probability estimation without sacrificing the model's inherent explanation-generation capabilities. Experimental results across multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that models fine-tuned with CLSGen outperform existing baselines in classification metrics (AUROC and F1-score). Regarding explanation, the results showed strong alignment between predicted labels and generated justifications, as well as high readability.
title_zh: CLSGen：一种用于联合概率分类与语言化解释的双头微调框架
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型（LLMs）的最新进展，人们越来越关注将这些模型应用于解决复杂且具有挑战性的问题。现代LLM具备处理长上下文和生成口语化解释的能力，在应对现实世界应用方面展现出巨大潜力。然而，将LLM用于实际决策时面临的一个关键障碍是其无法提供可靠、量化的概率估计。尽管采用传统判别式目标（类似于仅编码器模型）对LLM进行特定任务微调可生成概率估计，但这常常导致灾难性遗忘和语言崩溃现象，使模型丧失生成解释的能力，严重削弱其可解释性和可用性。为应对这一挑战，我们提出了CLSGen——一种专为二分类任务设计的新型LLM微调框架。CLSGen框架包含一种新的模型架构、训练方法以及数据构建策略，能够在不牺牲模型固有解释生成能力的前提下，实现稳健的概率估计。在多个基准数据集上的实验结果表明，经过CLSGen微调的模型在分类性能指标（AUROC和F1分数）上均优于现有基线方法。在解释生成方面，结果显示出预测标签与生成理由之间高度一致，且文本可读性良好。

title: $λ_A$: A Typed Lambda Calculus for LLM Agent Composition
authors: Qin Liu
abstract: Existing LLM agent frameworks lack formal semantics: there is no principled way to determine whether an agent configuration is well-formed or will terminate. We present $λ_A$, a typed lambda calculus for agent composition that extends the simply-typed lambda calculus with oracle calls, bounded fixpoints (the ReAct loop), probabilistic choice, and mutable environments. We prove type safety, termination of bounded fixpoints, and soundness of derived lint rules, with partial Coq mechanization (1,567 lines, 43 completed proofs). As a practical application, we derive a lint tool that detects structural configuration errors directly from the operational semantics. An evaluation on 835 real-world GitHub agent configurations shows that 94.1% are structurally incomplete under $λ_A$, with YAML-only lint precision at 54%, rising to 96--100% under joint YAML+Python AST analysis on 175 samples. This gap quantifies, for the first time, the degree of semantic entanglement between declarative configuration and imperative code in the agent ecosystem. We further show that five mainstream paradigms (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI SDK, Dify) embed as typed $λ_A$ fragments, establishing $λ_A$ as a unifying calculus for LLM agent composition.
title_zh: $λ_A$：一种用于大语言模型智能体组合的类型化lambda演算
abstract_zh: 现有的大语言模型智能体框架缺乏形式化语义：目前尚无系统性方法来判断智能体配置是否合法或是否会终止。我们提出 $λ_A$，一种用于智能体组合的类型化 lambda 演算，它在简单类型 lambda 演算的基础上扩展了预言机调用、有界不动点（ReAct 循环）、概率选择以及可变环境。我们证明了类型安全性、有界不动点的终止性，以及推导出的代码检查规则的正确性，并完成了部分 Coq 机械化验证（共 1,567 行代码，43 个已完成证明）。作为实际应用，我们基于操作语义推导出一个代码检查工具，可直接检测结构化的配置错误。对 835 个真实世界 GitHub 智能体配置的评估表明，在 $λ_A$ 语义下 94.1% 的配置存在结构性不完整问题；仅使用 YAML 的静态检查精度仅为 54%，而结合 YAML 与 Python AST 分析后，在 175 个样本上的精度提升至 96%–100%。这一差距首次量化了智能体生态中声明式配置与命令式代码之间的语义纠缠程度。此外，我们进一步证明了五种主流范式（LangGraph、CrewAI、AutoGen、OpenAI SDK、Dify）均可嵌入为 $λ_A$ 的有类型片段，确立了 $λ_A$ 作为大语言模型智能体组合的统一演算体系。

title: HiEdit: Lifelong Model Editing with Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yangfan Wang, Tianyang Sun, Chen Tang, Jie Liu, Wei Cai, Jingchi Jiang
abstract: Lifelong model editing (LME) aims to sequentially rectify outdated or inaccurate knowledge in deployed LLMs while minimizing side effects on unrelated inputs. However, existing approaches typically apply parameter perturbations to a static and dense set of LLM layers for all editing instances. This practice is counter-intuitive, as we hypothesize that different pieces of knowledge are stored in distinct layers of the model. Neglecting this layer-wise specificity can impede adaptability in integrating new knowledge and result in catastrophic forgetting for both general and previously edited knowledge. To address this, we propose HiEdit, a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that adaptively identifies the most knowledge-relevant layers for each editing instance. By enabling dynamic, instance-aware layer selection and incorporating an intrinsic reward for sparsity, HiEdit achieves precise, localized updates. Experiments on various LLMs show that HiEdit boosts the performance of the competitive RLEdit by an average of 8.48% with perturbing only half of the layers per edit. Our code is available at: https://github.com/yangfanww/hiedit.
title_zh: HiEdit：基于分层强化学习的终身模型编辑
abstract_zh: 终身模型编辑（LME）旨在顺序地修正已部署的大规模语言模型（LLM）中过时或不准确的知识，同时尽量减少对无关输入的副作用。然而，现有方法通常对所有编辑实例采用静态且密集的LLM层参数扰动，这种做法违背直觉——我们假设不同知识片段存储在模型的不同层级中。忽视这种分层特异性会阻碍新知识的灵活整合，并导致通用知识及先前已编辑知识的灾难性遗忘。为解决这一问题，我们提出HiEdit，一种分层强化学习框架，能够自适应地为每个编辑实例识别最相关的知识层。通过实现动态、实例感知的层选择，并引入稀疏性内在奖励机制，HiEdit实现了精准且局部化的更新。在多种LLM上的实验表明，与竞争性方法RLEdit相比，HiEdit在每次编辑仅扰动一半层数的情况下，平均性能提升达8.48%。代码已开源：https://github.com/yangfanww/hiedit。

title: CocoaBench: Evaluating Unified Digital Agents in the Wild
authors: CocoaBench Team, Shibo Hao, Zhining Zhang, Zhiqi Liang, Tianyang Liu, Yuheng Zha, Qiyue Gao, Jixuan Chen, Zilong Wang, Zhoujun Cheng, Haoxiang Zhang, Junli Wang, Hexi Jin, Boyuan Zheng, Kun Zhou, Yu Wang, Feng Yao, Licheng Liu, Yijiang Li, Zhifei Li, Zhengtao Han, Pracha Promthaw, Tommaso Cerruti, Xiaohan Fu, Ziqiao Ma, Jingbo Shang, Lianhui Qin, Julian McAuley, Eric P. Xing, Zhengzhong Liu, Rupesh Kumar Srivastava, Zhiting Hu
abstract: LLM agents now perform strongly in software engineering, deep research, GUI automation, and various other applications, while recent agent scaffolds and models are increasingly integrating these capabilities into unified systems. Yet, most evaluations still test these capabilities in isolation, which leaves a gap for more diverse use cases that require agents to combine different capabilities. We introduce CocoaBench, a benchmark for unified digital agents built from human-designed, long-horizon tasks that require flexible composition of vision, search, and coding. Tasks are specified only by an instruction and an automatic evaluation function over the final output, enabling reliable and scalable evaluation across diverse agent infrastructures. We also present CocoaAgent, a lightweight shared scaffold for controlled comparison across model backbones. Experiments show that current agents remain far from reliable on CocoaBench, with the best evaluated system achieving only 45.1% success rate. Our analysis further points to substantial room for improvement in reasoning and planning, tool use and execution, and visual grounding.
title_zh: CocoaBench：在真实环境中评估统一的数字代理
abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）代理在软件工程、深度研究、GUI自动化及其他多种应用中表现强劲，而近期的代理框架与模型正日益将这些能力整合进统一的系统中。然而，大多数评估仍仅孤立地测试各项能力，这导致在需要代理灵活组合多种技能的多样化应用场景中存在明显空白。为此，我们提出了CocoaBench——一个基于人类设计的、具有长周期特性的统一数字代理基准测试集，其任务要求代理灵活地融合视觉理解、信息检索与代码生成能力。所有任务仅通过指令和对最终输出的自动评估函数进行定义，从而可在多种代理基础设施上实现可靠且可扩展的评估。同时，我们还推出了CocoaAgent，一个轻量级的共享框架，用于在不同模型架构间进行可控对比。实验结果表明，当前代理在CocoaBench上的表现仍远未达到可靠水平，最佳系统仅达到45.1%的成功率。我们的分析进一步揭示了在推理与规划、工具使用与执行、视觉定位等方面仍有巨大提升空间。

title: Do Agent Rules Shape or Distort? Guardrails Beat Guidance in Coding Agents
authors: Xing Zhang, Guanghui Wang, Yanwei Cui, Wei Qiu, Ziyuan Li, Bing Zhu, Peiyang He
abstract: Developers increasingly guide AI coding agents through natural language instruction files (e.g., CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules), yet no controlled study has measured whether these rules actually improve agent performance or which properties make a rule beneficial. We scrape 679 such files (25,532 rules) from GitHub and conduct the first large-scale empirical evaluation, running over 5,000 agent runs with a state-of-the-art coding agent on SWE-bench Verified. Rules improve performance by 7--14 percentage points, but random rules help as much as expert-curated ones -- suggesting rules work through context priming rather than specific instruction. Negative constraints ("do not refactor unrelated code") are the only individually beneficial rule type, while positive directives ("follow code style") actively hurt -- a pattern we analyze through the lens of potential-based reward shaping (PBRS). Moreover, individual rules are mostly harmful in isolation yet collectively helpful, with no degradation up to 50 rules. These findings expose a hidden reliability risk -- well-intentioned rules routinely degrade agent performance -- and provide a clear principle for safe agent configuration: constrain what agents must not do, rather than prescribing what they should.
title_zh: 代理规则是塑造还是扭曲？护栏优于指引在编码代理中的应用
abstract_zh: 开发者越来越多地通过自然语言指令文件（如 CLAUDE.md、.cursorrules）来引导AI编程代理，但迄今为止尚无受控研究评估这些规则是否真正提升了代理性能，或哪些特性使规则更具价值。我们从 GitHub 上抓取了 679 个此类文件（共包含 25,532 条规则），并首次开展大规模实证评估，在 SWE-bench Verified 上使用最先进的编码代理运行了超过 5,000 次实验。结果显示，规则可使性能提升 7 至 14 个百分点，但随机生成的规则与专家精心设计的规则效果相当——这表明规则的作用主要源于上下文提示（context priming），而非具体指令本身。负向约束（如“不要重构无关代码”）是唯一具有独立正向效益的规则类型，而正向指令（如“遵循代码风格”）反而会带来负面影响——这一现象我们通过基于潜在奖励的奖励塑造（Potential-Based Reward Shaping, PBRS）理论进行了分析。此外，单条规则在孤立状态下大多有害，但集体使用时却显著有益，且在最多达 50 条规则的情况下未出现性能下降。这些发现揭示了一个隐藏的可靠性风险：看似善意的规则常常会降低代理性能。由此得出一个明确的安全配置原则：应限制代理不得做什么，而非规定它们必须做什么。

title: Rethinking Token-Level Credit Assignment in RLVR: A Polarity-Entropy Analysis
authors: Yuhang He, Haodong Wu, Siyi Liu, Hongyu Ge, Hange Zhou, Keyi Wu, Zhuo Zheng, Qihong Lin, Zixin Zhong, Yongqi Zhang
abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has substantially improved the reasoning ability of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, its sparse outcome-based rewards pose a fundamental credit assignment problem. We analyze this problem through the joint lens of reward polarity and token entropy. Our diagnostic tool, the Four Quadrant Decomposition, isolates token updates by polarity and entropy, and controlled ablations show that reasoning improvements concentrate in the high-entropy quadrants. To justify this observation theoretically, we adapt Conditional Mutual Information to the autoregressive RLVR setting and prove that the credit a token can carry is upper-bounded by its entropy. This view yields testable predictions that reasoning gains arise primarily from high-entropy tokens, with unique roles for positive and negative updates. A gradient analysis of GRPO further reveals how uniform reward broadcast dilutes signal at high-entropy positions while over-crediting deterministic tokens. Grounded in these insights, we propose Entropy-Aware Policy Optimization (EAPO) that modulates token-level learning signals accordingly. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EAPO outperforms strong baselines across two model families.
title_zh: 重新思考强化学习视觉问答中的令牌级信用分配：极性-熵分析
abstract_zh: 基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）显著提升了大型语言模型（LLMs）的推理能力。然而，其稀疏的结果导向奖励带来了根本性的信用分配难题。本文从奖励极性和词元熵的联合视角分析该问题。我们提出的诊断工具——四象限分解法，能够根据极性和熵对词元更新进行分离，受控消融实验表明，推理性能的提升主要集中在高熵象限。为从理论上解释这一现象，我们将条件互信息引入自回归RLVR框架，并证明单个词元所能承载的信用上限由其熵决定。这一理论视角提出了可检验的预测：推理增益主要来自高熵词元，且正负更新各自扮演独特角色。对GRPO的梯度分析进一步揭示，均匀的奖励广播会削弱高熵位置的信号，同时过度赋予确定性词元信用。基于上述洞见，我们提出熵感知策略优化（EAPO），能够相应地调节词元级别的学习信号。大量实验证明，EAPO在两大模型族上均显著优于现有强基线方法。

title: A Categorical Basis for Robust Program Analysis
authors: Zachary Kincaid, Shaowei Zhu
abstract: Users of program analyses expect that results change predictably in response to changes in their programs, but many analyses fail to provide such robustness. This paper introduces a theoretical framework that provides a unified language to articulate robustness properties. By modeling programs and their properties as objects in a category, diverse notions of robustness-from variable renaming to semantic refinement and structural transformation-can be characterized as structure-preserving functors. Beyond formulating the meaning of robustness, this paper provides methods for achieving it. The first is a general recipe for designing robust analyses, by lifting a sound and robust analysis from a restricted (sub-Turing) model of computation to a sound and robust analysis for general programs. This recipe demystifies the design of several existing loop summarization and termination analyses by showing they are instantiations of this general recipe, and furthermore elucidates their robustness properties. The second is a characterization of a sense in which an algebraic program analysis is robust, provided that it is comprised of robust operators. In particular, we show that such analyses behave predictably under common refactoring patterns, such as variable renaming and loop unrolling.
title_zh: 稳健程序分析的范畴基础
abstract_zh: 程序分析的使用者期望，当其程序发生变化时，分析结果能够以可预测的方式随之改变，但许多现有的分析方法无法提供这种稳健性。本文提出一个理论框架，为表达稳健性属性提供了一个统一的语言。通过将程序及其属性建模为范畴中的对象，各种不同的稳健性概念——从变量重命名到语义细化和结构变换——都可以被刻画为保持结构的函子。除了阐明稳健性的含义之外，本文还提供了实现稳健性的方法。第一种方法是一种设计稳健分析的通用方案：将一个在受限（非图灵完备）计算模型上既正确又稳健的分析，提升为对一般程序同样正确且稳健的分析。该方案揭示了若干现有循环摘要和终止性分析的设计原理，表明它们都是这一通用方案的具体实例，并进一步阐明了它们的稳健性特征。第二种方法则给出了代数程序分析稳健性的刻画条件：只要该分析由稳健的算子构成，它就具备某种意义上的稳健性。特别地，我们证明了这类分析在常见的重构模式下（如变量重命名、循环展开）会表现出可预测的行为。

title: MAFIG: Multi-agent Driven Formal Instruction Generation Framework
authors: Shixing Zhao, Zheng Si, Pengpeng Ouyang, Zhengqing Hu, Wanqi Zhu, Dong Chen, Yibo Guo, Mingliang Xu
abstract: Emergency situations in scheduling systems often trigger local functional failures that undermine system stability and even cause system collapse. Existing methods primarily rely on robust scheduling or reactive scheduling, handling emergencies through predefined rules or rescheduling strategies. However, the diversity and unpredictability of real-world emergencies make them difficult to anticipate, which limits the adaptability of these methods in complex scenarios. Recent studies have shown that Large Language Models (LLMs) possess strong potential for complex scheduling tasks because of their extensive prior knowledge and strong reasoning capabilities. Nevertheless, the high inference latency of LLMs and the lengthy contextual information of scheduling systems significantly hinder their application for emergency handling. To mitigate these issues, we propose the Multi-agent Driven Formal Instruction Generation Framework (MAFIG). The framework constrains the decision scope to local functional modules affected by emergency situations and repairs scheduling logic rapidly by generating formal instructions. MAFIG contains a Perception Agent and an Emergency Decision Agent, which mitigates the adverse impact of lengthy system contexts on emergency decision-making. We further introduce span-focused loss-driven local distillation mechanism (SFL) to transfer the decision-making capability of powerful Cloud Large Language Models (C-LLMs) to lightweight local models, reducing inference latency while preserving decision-making effectiveness. Experiments in the Port, Warehousing, and Deck scheduling datasets show success rates of 98.49\%, 94.97\%, and 97.50\%, with average processing times of 0.33 s, 0.23 s, and 0.19 s. These results demonstrate that MAFIG effectively mitigates the impact of emergencies and improves the robustness and adaptability of scheduling systems.
title_zh: MAFIG：多智能体驱动的形式化指令生成框架
abstract_zh: 调度系统中的紧急情况常常引发局部功能故障，从而威胁系统稳定性，甚至导致系统崩溃。现有方法主要依赖于鲁棒调度或反应式调度，通过预设规则或重调度策略来应对突发事件。然而，现实世界中紧急情况的多样性和不可预测性使得其难以提前预见，这限制了这些方法在复杂场景下的适应能力。近年来的研究表明，大型语言模型（LLMs）因其丰富的先验知识和强大的推理能力，在处理复杂调度任务方面展现出巨大潜力。然而，LLMs较高的推理延迟以及调度系统中冗长的上下文信息，严重制约了其在应急处理中的应用。为缓解上述问题，我们提出了多智能体驱动的形式化指令生成框架（MAFIG）。该框架将决策范围限定在受紧急情况影响的局部功能模块，并通过生成形式化指令快速修复调度逻辑。MAFIG包含感知智能体（Perception Agent）与应急决策智能体（Emergency Decision Agent），有效降低了长系统上下文对应急决策的负面影响。此外，我们进一步提出基于跨度聚焦的损失驱动局部蒸馏机制（SFL），将强大云端大语言模型（C-LLMs）的决策能力高效迁移至轻量级本地模型，显著降低推理延迟，同时保持决策有效性。在港口、仓储和甲板调度数据集上的实验结果显示，成功率达到98.49%、94.97%和97.50%，平均处理时间分别为0.33秒、0.23秒和0.19秒。结果表明，MAFIG能够有效缓解紧急事件的影响，显著提升调度系统的鲁棒性与适应性。

title: Taking a Pulse on How Generative AI is Reshaping the Software Engineering Research Landscape
authors: Bianca Trinkenreich, Fabio Calefato, Kelly Blincoe, Viggo Tellefsen Wivestad, Antonio Pedro Santos Alves, Júlia Condé Araújo, Marina Condé Araújo, Paolo Tell, Marcos Kalinowski, Thomas Zimmermann, Margaret-Anne Storey
abstract: Context: Software engineering (SE) researchers increasingly study Generative AI (GenAI) while also incorporating it into their own research practices. Despite rapid adoption, there is limited empirical evidence on how GenAI is used in SE research and its implications for research practices and governance. Aims: We conduct a large-scale survey of 457 SE researchers publishing in top venues between 2023 and 2025. Method: Using quantitative and qualitative analyses, we examine who uses GenAI and why, where it is used across research activities, and how researchers perceive its benefits, opportunities, challenges, risks, and governance. Results: GenAI use is widespread, with many researchers reporting pressure to adopt and align their work with it. Usage is concentrated in writing and early-stage activities, while methodological and analytical tasks remain largely human-driven. Although productivity gains are widely perceived, concerns about trust, correctness, and regulatory uncertainty persist. Researchers highlight risks such as inaccuracies and bias, emphasize mitigation through human oversight and verification, and call for clearer governance, including guidance on responsible use and peer review. Conclusion: We provide a fine-grained, SE-specific characterization of GenAI use across research activities, along with taxonomies of GenAI use cases for research and peer review, opportunities, risks, mitigation strategies, and governance needs. These findings establish an empirical baseline for the responsible integration of GenAI into academic practice.
title_zh: 探析生成式AI如何重塑软件工程研究格局
abstract_zh:
背景：软件工程（SE）领域的研究人员越来越多地研究生成式人工智能（GenAI），同时也在其自身研究实践中逐步引入该技术。尽管GenAI的采用速度迅速，但目前关于其在SE研究中的实际应用情况及其对研究实践与治理影响的实证证据仍十分有限。

目标：本研究对2023至2025年间在顶级学术会议和期刊发表论文的457名SE研究人员进行了大规模调查。

方法：通过定量与定性相结合的分析方法，考察了哪些研究人员使用GenAI及其原因，GenAI在研究活动各环节中的具体应用场景，以及研究人员对其带来的效益、机遇、挑战、风险及治理需求的看法。

结果：GenAI的使用已相当普遍，许多研究人员表示面临必须采纳并使自身工作与GenAI保持一致的压力。其应用主要集中在写作及研究初期阶段，而方法设计与数据分析等核心环节仍以人工为主。尽管普遍认为GenAI带来了生产效率的提升，但对可信度、准确性以及监管不确定性的担忧依然存在。研究人员指出，潜在风险包括内容不准确和偏见问题，强调应通过人工监督与验证来加以缓解，并呼吁建立更清晰的治理框架，包括负责任使用指南以及在同行评审中纳入相关规范。

结论：本研究为GenAI在SE研究活动中的应用提供了细致入微、领域特定的刻画，构建了涵盖研究与同行评审中GenAI使用场景、机遇、风险、缓解策略及治理需求的分类体系。这些发现为GenAI在学术实践中的负责任整合奠定了实证基础。

title: UniToolCall: Unifying Tool-Use Representation, Data, and Evaluation for LLM Agents
authors: Yijuan Liang, Xinghao Chen, Yifan Ge, Ziyi Wu, Hao Wu, Changyu Zeng, Wei Xing, Xiaoyu Shen
abstract: Tool-use capability is a fundamental component of LLM agents, enabling them to interact with external systems through structured function calls. However, existing research exhibits inconsistent interaction representations, largely overlooks the structural distribution of tool-use trajectories, and relies on incompatible evaluation benchmarks. We present UniToolCall, a unified framework for tool learning that standardizes the entire pipeline from toolset construction and dataset generation to evaluation. The framework curates a large tool pool of 22k+ tools and constructs a hybrid training corpus of 390k+ instances by combining 10 standardized public datasets with structurally controlled synthetic trajectories. It explicitly models diverse interaction patterns, including single-hop vs. multi-hop and single-turn vs. multi-turn, while capturing both serial and parallel execution structures. To support coherent multi-turn reasoning, we further introduce an Anchor Linkage mechanism that enforces cross-turn dependencies. Furthermore, we convert 7 public benchmarks into a unified Query--Action--Observation--Answer (QAOA) representation with fine-grained evaluation at the function-call, turn, and conversation levels. Experiments show that fine-tuning Qwen3-8B on our dataset substantially improves tool-use performance. Under the distractor-heavy Hybrid-20 setting, achieves 93.0% single-turn Strict Precision, outperforming commercial models including GPT, Gemini, and Claude.
title_zh: UniToolCall：统一大语言模型智能体的工具使用表征、数据与评估
abstract_zh: 工具使用能力是大型语言模型（LLM）智能体的核心组成部分，使其能够通过结构化的函数调用与外部系统进行交互。然而，现有研究在交互表示上存在不一致，普遍忽视了工具使用轨迹的结构分布特征，并依赖于互不兼容的评估基准。为此，我们提出 UniToolCall——一个统一的工具学习框架，旨在从工具集构建、数据集生成到评估的全流程实现标准化。该框架汇聚了超过2.2万个工具的大型工具库，并通过结合10个标准化公开数据集与结构可控的合成轨迹，构建了一个包含39万+样本的混合训练语料库。框架显式建模了多种交互模式，包括单跳与多跳、单轮与多轮交互，并同时捕捉串行与并行执行结构。为支持连贯的多轮推理，我们进一步引入了“锚点关联”（Anchor Linkage）机制，以强制跨轮次的依赖关系。此外，我们将7个公开基准统一转换为细粒度的“查询-动作-观察-回答”（Query--Action--Observation--Answer, QAOA）格式，并在函数调用级、轮次级和对话级实现精细化评估。实验表明，在我们的数据集上对Qwen3-8B进行微调，显著提升了其工具使用性能：在干扰密集的Hybrid-20测试场景下，单轮严格精确率达到93.0%，优于包括GPT、Gemini和Claude在内的多个商用模型。

title: Escaping the Context Bottleneck: Active Context Curation for LLM Agents via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xiaozhe Li, Tianyi Lyu, Yizhao Yang, Liang Shan, Siyi Yang, Ligao Zhang, Zhuoyi Huang, Qingwen Liu, Yang Li
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle with long-horizon tasks due to the "context bottleneck" and the "lost-in-the-middle" phenomenon, where accumulated noise from verbose environments degrades reasoning over multi-turn interactions. To address this issue, we introduce a symbiotic framework that decouples context management from task execution. Our architecture pairs a lightweight, specialized policy model, ContextCurator, with a powerful frozen foundation model, TaskExecutor. Trained via reinforcement learning, ContextCurator actively reduces information entropy in the working memory. It aggressively prunes environmental noise while preserving reasoning anchors, that is, sparse data points that are critical for future deductions. On WebArena, our framework improves the success rate of Gemini-3.0-flash from 36.4% to 41.2% while reducing token consumption by 8.8% (from 47.4K to 43.3K). On DeepSearch, it achieves a 57.1% success rate, compared with 53.9%, while reducing token consumption by a factor of 8. Remarkably, a 7B ContextCurator matches the context management performance of GPT-4o, providing a scalable and computationally efficient paradigm for autonomous long-horizon agents.
title_zh: 突破上下文瓶颈：基于强化学习的LLM智能体主动上下文优化
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在处理长周期任务时面临“上下文瓶颈”和“中间迷失”现象，即冗长环境中的累积噪声会降低多轮交互中的推理能力。为解决这一问题，我们提出一种共生式框架，将上下文管理与任务执行解耦。该架构结合了一个轻量级、专用的策略模型——ContextCurator，以及一个强大的冻结基础模型——TaskExecutor。通过强化学习进行训练，ContextCurator 能够主动降低工作记忆中的信息熵，有效剔除环境噪声，同时保留关键的推理锚点——即对未来推断至关重要的稀疏数据点。在 WebArena 上，该框架将 Gemini-3.0-flash 的成功率从 36.4% 提升至 41.2%，同时 token 消耗减少 8.8%（从 47.4K 降至 43.3K）。在 DeepSearch 上，成功率达到 57.1%，高于基准的 53.9%，且 token 消耗降低至原来的八分之一。尤为突出的是，一个仅 7B 规模的 ContextCurator 即可达到 GPT-4o 的上下文管理性能，为自主长周期智能体提供了一种可扩展且计算高效的范式。

title: From Agent Loops to Structured Graphs:A Scheduler-Theoretic Framework for LLM Agent Execution
authors: Hu Wei
abstract: The dominant paradigm for building LLM based agents is the Agent Loop, an iterative cycle where a single language model decides what to do next by reading an ever growing context window. This paradigm has three structural weaknesses: implicit dependencies between steps, unbounded recovery loops, and mutable execution history that complicates debugging. We characterize the Agent Loop as a single ready unit scheduler: at any moment, at most one executable unit is active, and the choice of which unit to activate comes from opaque LLM inference rather than an inspectable policy. This perspective places Agent Loops and graph based execution engines on a single semantic continuum. We propose SGH, Structured Graph Harness, which lifts control flow from implicit context into an explicit static DAG. SGH makes three commitments: execution plans are immutable within a plan version, planning execution and recovery are separated into three layers, and recovery follows a strict escalation protocol. These choices trade some expressiveness for controllability, verifiability, and implementability. Our contributions are fourfold: a scheduler unified framework that applies classical scheduling theory to LLM agent execution and identifies challenges introduced by non deterministic LLM nodes; a trade off analysis of controllability, expressiveness, and implementability across 70 surveyed systems; a formal specification including a node state machine with termination and soundness guarantees; and an attributable experimental framework with a seven group design for future validation. This is a position paper and design proposal. We provide a theoretical framework, design analysis, and experimental protocol, not a production implementation or empirical results.
title_zh: 从代理循环到结构化图：一种基于调度理论的大型语言模型代理执行框架
abstract_zh:
基于大语言模型（LLM）构建智能体的主流范式是“智能体循环”（Agent Loop），这是一种迭代循环机制，其中单一语言模型通过不断读取日益增长的上下文窗口来决定下一步行动。这一范式存在三个结构性缺陷：步骤间的隐式依赖、无界恢复循环，以及可变的执行历史，这使得调试变得复杂。我们将Agent Loop视为一种“单一就绪单元调度器”：在任何时刻，最多只有一个可执行单元处于激活状态，而选择哪个单元激活则来自语言模型的不透明推理过程，而非可检查的策略。从这一视角出发，Agent Loop与基于图的执行引擎被置于同一语义连续体上。

我们提出SGH（Structured Graph Harness，结构化图调度框架），将控制流从隐式的上下文环境中显式地提升至静态有向无环图（DAG）中。SGH做出三项核心承诺：在特定计划版本内，执行计划不可变；规划、执行与恢复被划分为三个独立层级；恢复遵循严格的升级协议。这些设计选择在一定程度上牺牲了表达能力，以换取更高的可控性、可验证性和可实现性。

我们的贡献包括四个方面：  
1. 一个统一的调度器框架，将经典调度理论应用于LLM智能体执行，并识别出非确定性LLM节点带来的挑战；  
2. 对70个调研系统进行的权衡分析，涵盖可控性、表达能力和可实现性之间的平衡关系；  
3. 一套形式化规范，包含具有终止保证和正确性保障的节点状态机；  
4. 一个可追溯的实验框架，采用七组设计，为未来验证提供支持。

本文为立场论文与设计提案，我们提供的是理论框架、设计分析与实验协议，而非生产级实现或实证结果。

title: Do LLMs Know Tool Irrelevance? Demystifying Structural Alignment Bias in Tool Invocations
authors: Yilong Liu, Xixun Lin, Pengfei Cao, Ge Zhang, Fang Fang, Yanan Cao
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in utilizing external tools. In practice, however, LLMs are often exposed to tools that are irrelevant to the user's query, in which case the desired behavior is to refrain from invocations. In this work, we identify a widespread yet overlooked mechanistic flaw in tool refusal, which we term structural alignment bias: Even when a tool fails to serve the user's goal, LLMs still tend to invoke it whenever query attributes can be validly assigned to tool parameters. To systematically study this bias, we introduce SABEval, a new dataset that decouples structural alignment from semantic relevance. Our analysis shows that structural alignment bias induces severe tool-invocation errors in LLMs, yet remains largely unaccounted for in existing evaluations. To investigate the internal mechanisms underlying this bias, we propose Contrastive Attention Attribution, which reveals two competing pathways for semantic checking and structural matching. The relative strength of these pathways drives LLMs' tool invocation decisions. Based on these findings, we further introduce a rebalancing strategy that effectively mitigates structural alignment bias, as demonstrated by extensive experiments, without degrading general tool-use capabilities.
title_zh: 大语言模型是否知晓工具无关性？揭秘工具调用中的结构对齐偏差
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在利用外部工具方面展现了令人印象深刻的能力。然而，在实际应用中，LLMs 常常会接触到与用户查询无关的工具，此时理想的行为应为避免调用这些工具。本文揭示了一种广泛存在但被忽视的机制性缺陷——结构对齐偏差：即使某个工具无法实现用户的目标，只要查询中的某些属性能够被合理地映射到工具参数上，LLMs 仍倾向于调用该工具。为了系统地研究这一偏差，我们提出了 SABEval，一个将结构对齐与语义相关性解耦的新数据集。我们的分析表明，结构对齐偏差会导致 LLMs 产生严重的工具调用错误，但在现有评估体系中却几乎未被充分考虑。为进一步探究该偏差背后的内在机制，我们提出了对比注意力归因方法（Contrastive Attention Attribution），该方法揭示了语义检查与结构匹配之间存在的两条竞争路径，而这两条路径的相对强度决定了 LLMs 的工具调用决策。基于上述发现，我们进一步提出一种再平衡策略，通过大量实验验证，该策略能有效缓解结构对齐偏差，同时不损害模型的一般工具使用能力。

title: The Past Is Not Past: Memory-Enhanced Dynamic Reward Shaping
authors: Yang Liu, Enxi Wang, Yufei Gao, Weixin Zhang, Bo Wang, Zhiyuan Zeng, Yikai Zhang, Yining Zheng, Xipeng Qiu
abstract: Despite the success of reinforcement learning for large language models, a common failure mode is reduced sampling diversity, where the policy repeatedly generates similar erroneous behaviors. Classical entropy regularization encourages randomness under the current policy, but does not explicitly discourage recurrent failure patterns across rollouts. We propose MEDS, a Memory-Enhanced Dynamic reward Shaping framework that incorporates historical behavioral signals into reward design. By storing and leveraging intermediate model representations, we capture features of past rollouts and use density-based clustering to identify frequently recurring error patterns. Rollouts assigned to more prevalent error clusters are penalized more heavily, encouraging broader exploration while reducing repeated mistakes. Across five datasets and three base models, MEDS consistently improves average performance over existing baselines, achieving gains of up to 4.13 pass@1 points and 4.37 pass@128 points. Additional analyses using both LLM-based annotations and quantitative diversity metrics show that MEDS increases behavioral diversity during sampling.
title_zh: 过去并非过去：增强记忆的动态奖励塑造
abstract_zh: 尽管强化学习在大型语言模型中取得了成功，但一个常见的失败模式是采样多样性降低，即策略反复生成相似的错误行为。传统的熵正则化虽然能在当前策略下鼓励随机性，却并未明确抑制跨多次采样过程中的重复错误模式。为此，我们提出了MEDS——一种基于记忆增强的动态奖励塑形框架，将历史行为信号融入奖励设计之中。通过存储并利用中间模型表示，我们能够捕捉过往采样的特征，并采用基于密度的聚类方法识别出频繁出现的错误模式。对于被分配到更常见错误簇的采样轨迹，系统施加更严厉的惩罚，从而促进更广泛的探索，同时减少重复错误的发生。在五个数据集和三种基础模型上的实验表明，MEDS始终优于现有基线方法，性能提升最高达4.13个pass@1点和4.37个pass@128点。进一步的分析，结合基于大语言模型的标注与定量多样性指标，也证实了MEDS在采样过程中显著提升了行为多样性。

title: CapBench: A Multi-PDK Dataset for Machine-Learning-Based Post-Layout Capacitance Extraction
authors: Hector R. Rodriguez, Jiechen Huang, Wenjian Yu
abstract: We present CapBench, a fully reproducible, multi-PDK dataset for capacitance extraction. The dataset is derived from open-source designs, including single-core CPUs, systems-on-chip, and media accelerators. All designs are fully placed and routed using 14 independent OpenROAD flow runs spanning three technology nodes: ASAP7, NanGate45, and Sky130HD. From these layouts, we extract 61,855 3D windows across three size tiers to enable transfer learning and scalability studies. High-fidelity capacitance labels are generated using RWCap, a state-of-the-art random-walk solver, and validated against the industry-standard Raphael, achieving a mean absolute error of 0.64% for total capacitance. Each window is pre-processed into density maps, graph representations, and point clouds. We evaluate 10 machine learning architectures that illustrate dataset usage and serve as baselines, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs), point cloud transformers, and graph neural networks (GNNs). CNNs demonstrate the lowest errors (1.75%), while GNNs are up to 41.4x faster but exhibit larger errors (10.2%), illustrating a clear accuracy-speed trade-off. Code and dataset are available at https://github.com/THU-numbda/CapBench.
title_zh: CapBench：一个用于基于机器学习的布局后电容提取的多PDK数据集
abstract_zh: 我们提出CapBench，这是一个完全可复现、面向多PDK（工艺设计套件）的电容提取数据集。该数据集源自开源设计，涵盖单核CPU、系统级芯片（SoC）以及媒体加速器等典型电路。所有设计均通过14次独立的OpenROAD流程运行完成布局布线，覆盖三个工艺节点：ASAP7、NanGate45和Sky130HD。基于这些版图，我们提取了61,855个三维窗口，分为三个尺寸层级，以支持迁移学习与可扩展性研究。高保真电容标签由业界领先的随机游走求解器RWCap生成，并与工业标准工具Raphael进行对比验证，总电容的平均绝对误差仅为0.64%。每个窗口均已预处理为密度图、图结构表示和点云数据。我们评估了10种机器学习架构，展示了该数据集的应用潜力并提供了基准性能，包括卷积神经网络（CNN）、点云Transformer以及图神经网络（GNN）。实验结果表明，CNN表现最佳，误差最低（1.75%）；而GNN在速度上最高可达41.4倍于CNN，但误差较大（10.2%），清晰地体现了精度与速度之间的权衡关系。代码与数据集已公开，获取地址为：https://github.com/THU-numbda/CapBench。

title: Technology solutions targeting the performance of gen-AI inference in resource constrained platforms
authors: Joyjit Kundu, Joshua Klein, Aakash Patel, Dwaipayan Biswas
abstract: The rise of generative AI workloads, particularly language model inference, is intensifying on/off-chip memory pressure. Multimodal inputs such as video streams or images and downstream applications like Question Answering (QA) and analysis over large documents incur long context lengths, requiring caching of massive Key and Value states of the previous tokens. Even a low degree of concurrent inference serving on resource-constrained devices, like mobiles, can further add to memory capacity pressure and runtime memory management complexity. In this paper, we evaluate the performance implications of two emerging technology solutions to alleviate the memory pressure in terms of both capacity and bandwidth using a hierarchical roofline-based analytical performance model. For large models (e.g., 13B parameters) and context lengths, we investigate the performance implications of High Bandwidth Storage (HBS) and outline bandwidth/latency requirements to achieve an acceptable throughput for interactivity. For small models (e.g., 1B parameters), we evaluate the merit of a bonded global buffer memory chiplet and propose how to best utilize it.
title_zh: 针对资源受限平台上生成式人工智能推理性能的技术解决方案
abstract_zh: 生成式AI工作负载，尤其是语言模型推理的兴起，正加剧片上与片外内存的压力。多模态输入（如视频流或图像）以及下游应用（如问答系统和大文档分析）往往需要处理长上下文长度，这要求缓存大量先前标记的Key和Value状态。即使在资源受限的设备（如移动设备）上仅进行低并发的推理服务，也会进一步增加内存容量压力，并提升运行时内存管理的复杂性。本文通过基于分层屋顶线（roofline）的分析性能模型，评估了两种新兴技术解决方案在缓解内存压力（包括容量与带宽方面）方面的性能影响。针对大规模模型（例如130亿参数）和长上下文长度场景，我们研究了高带宽存储（HBS）的性能影响，并明确了实现可接受交互吞吐量所需的带宽与延迟要求。对于小型模型（例如10亿参数），我们评估了集成全局缓冲内存小芯片（bonded global buffer memory chiplet）的优势，并提出了其最优使用方法。

title: Problem Reductions at Scale: Agentic Integration of Computationally Hard Problems
authors: Xi-Wei Pan, Shi-Wen An, Jin-Guo Liu
abstract: Solving an NP-hard optimization problem often requires reformulating it for a specific solver -- quantum hardware, a commercial optimizer, or a domain heuristic. A tool for polynomial-time reductions between hard problems would let practitioners route any supported problem to any supported solver through a single interface. Building such a library at scale, however, has remained out of reach. We show that harness engineering, the practice of designing constraints, verification systems, and feedback loops that channel AI coding agents, can overcome this barrier. Our harness combines a no-code contribution route for domain experts, a multilayer verification stack ranging from type-level checks to agentic feature tests (AI agents role-playing as end users), and a fully automated implementation-review-integration pipeline. In about three months, we built a command-line tool backed by a library of 100+ problem types and 200+~reduction rules in over 170k lines of Rust. The result suggests that a well-engineered harness lets agents build well-tested software at a scale and pace beyond prior reduction-library efforts. Because the reduction graph composes transitively, a new solver registered for any single problem type instantly becomes available to every problem connected by a reduction path. The source code is available at https://github.com/CodingThrust/problem-reductions.
title_zh: 大规模问题约简：计算难题的智能集成
