abstract_zh: 解决一个NP难优化问题通常需要针对特定求解器（如量子硬件、商业优化器或领域启发式方法）进行重新建模。如果存在一种可在困难问题间进行多项式时间归约的工具，从业者便可通过单一接口将任意支持的问题路由到任意支持的求解器。然而，要大规模构建这样的库一直遥不可及。我们发现，通过“约束工程”——即设计约束条件、验证系统和反馈回路，以引导AI编码代理——可以突破这一障碍。我们的约束系统结合了面向领域专家的无代码贡献方式，多层次的验证体系（从类型级检查到由AI代理扮演最终用户进行的智能功能测试），以及完全自动化的实现-审查-集成流水线。仅用约三个月时间，我们就开发出一个命令行工具，其背后是一个包含100多个问题类型和200多条归约规则的库，代码量超过17万行Rust语言代码。结果表明，经过精心设计的约束系统，使AI代理能够以远超以往归约库努力的规模与速度构建出经过充分测试的软件。由于归约图具有传递性，只要为任意一个问题类型注册了一个新求解器，该求解器即可立即对所有通过归约路径连接的问题可用。源代码已公开，地址为：https://github.com/CodingThrust/problem-reductions。

title: Three Roles, One Model: Role Orchestration at Inference Time to Close the Performance Gap Between Small and Large Agents
authors: S. Aaron McClendon, Jorge Gallego-Feliciano, Stavros Zervoudakis, Antonios Saravanos
abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents show promise on realistic tool-use tasks, but deploying capable agents on modest hardware remains challenging. We study whether inference-time scaffolding alone, without any additional training compute, can improve the performance of a small model in complex multi-step environments. Operating on a single 24\,GB GPU, we evaluate Qwen3-8B under both full-precision (FP16, 12K context) and 4-bit quantized (AWQ, 32K context) configurations. Without any intervention, the raw model achieves just 5.4\% (FP16) and 3.0\% (AWQ) task goal completion. Guided by a systematic failure mode analysis, we introduce a three-tier inference scaffolding pipeline that deploys the same frozen model in three distinct roles: (1) a summarization model that preserves critical artifacts (tokens, credentials, API responses) while compressing dialogue history; (2) the main agent model that reasons over the compressed context; and (3) an isolated correction model that reviews and revises the agent's code output without access to conversation history, breaking repetitive failure loops. Applied to the same unmodified model, this scaffolding yields 8.9\% (FP16) and 5.9\% (AWQ) task goal completion, roughly doubling performance in both settings, with particularly strong gains on difficulty-1 tasks (15.8\%$\to$26.3\% FP16; 5.3\%$\to$14.0\% AWQ). On full-precision inference, our scaffolded 8B model surpasses DeepSeek-Coder 33B Instruct (7.1\%) from the original AppWorld evaluation, demonstrating that structured inference-time interventions can make small models competitive with systems 4$\times$ their size. We formalize the approach as a scaffolded policy over a frozen base model, three invocations of the same weights with different conditioning, drawing connections to test-time compute scaling and action-space shaping in reinforcement learning.
title_zh: 三个角色，一个模型：推理时的角色编排以缩小小型与大型智能体之间的性能差距
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）代理在现实世界的工具使用任务中展现出巨大潜力，但在资源有限的硬件上部署高性能代理仍面临挑战。本文研究了仅通过推理时的辅助结构（inference-time scaffolding），不依赖额外训练计算资源，是否能够提升小型模型在复杂多步骤环境中的表现。我们在单块24 GB GPU上评估了Qwen3-8B模型在两种配置下的性能：全精度（FP16，支持12K上下文）和4位量化（AWQ，支持32K上下文）。在未进行任何干预的情况下，原始模型的任务目标完成率仅为5.4%（FP16）和3.0%（AWQ）。基于系统性的失败模式分析，我们提出了一种三层推理辅助流水线，将同一冻结的模型以三种不同角色协同使用：（1）摘要模型，用于压缩对话历史的同时保留关键信息（如令牌、凭证、API响应）；（2）主代理模型，在压缩后的上下文中进行推理；（3）独立纠错模型，审查并修正代理生成的代码输出，但不访问对话历史，从而打破重复性错误循环。在相同未修改的模型上应用该辅助结构后，任务目标完成率分别提升至8.9%（FP16）和5.9%（AWQ），性能几乎翻倍，尤其在难度等级为1的任务上提升显著（FP16从15.8%提升至26.3%，AWQ从5.3%提升至14.0%）。在全精度推理下，我们经过辅助的8B模型在原始AppWorld评测中超越了DeepSeek-Coder 33B Instruct（7.1%），证明了结构化的推理时干预手段可使小型模型在性能上媲美规模大四倍的系统。我们进一步将该方法形式化为对冻结基础模型的“辅助策略”（scaffolded policy），即通过三次调用同一组权重、施加不同条件，实现差异化功能，这一思路与强化学习中的测试时计算量扩展（test-time compute scaling）及动作空间塑造（action-space shaping）存在深刻关联。

title: Automated SVA Generation with LLMs
authors: Lik Tung Fu, Qihang Wang, Shaokai Ren, Mengli Zhang, Sichao Yang, Jun Liu, Xi Wang
abstract: Functional verification remains a dominant cost in modern IC development, and SystemVerilog Assertions (SVAs) are critical for simulation-based monitoring and formal property checking. However, writing SVAs by hand is time-consuming and error-prone. Directly prompting general-purpose large language models (LLMs) is also unreliable: the generated properties are often syntactically invalid or semantically incorrect, and the problem is exacerbated by scarce, high-quality domain training data. We present SVA Generator, a data-centric framework that translates natural-language SVA Descriptions (SVADs) into executable SVAs. It uses AST-grounded constraint injection and an automated supervision pipeline that enforces structural consistency and reduces hallucinations via de-duplication and constraint checks. To enable rigorous evaluation, we introduce a benchmark suite stratified by AST depth and use formal property equivalence checking to quantify semantic correctness separately from syntax validity, by checking mutual implication between the generated and reference properties under the same clocking and environment assumptions. Across all difficulty tiers, SVA Generator achieves comparable Syntax Pass Rate (SPR) to strong general LLM baselines, while delivering substantially higher Semantic Equivalence Rate (SER) on deeper tiers: +24.5 pp on D2, +26.0 pp on D3, and +17.5 pp on D4 relative to the best-performing general LLM, corresponding to a +22.7 pp SER improvement on average over D2--D4. These results highlight that high-fidelity data construction and depth-stratified benchmarking are key to reliable, semantics-preserving SVA generation.
title_zh: 使用大语言模型进行自动化SVA生成
abstract_zh: 功能验证仍然是现代集成电路（IC）开发中占主导地位的成本来源，而SystemVerilog断言（SVAs）在基于仿真的监控和形式化属性检查中起着关键作用。然而，手动编写SVAs耗时且容易出错。直接调用通用大语言模型（LLMs）也不可靠：生成的属性往往存在语法错误或语义不正确的问题，而这一问题在缺乏高质量领域训练数据的情况下更加严重。我们提出了SVA Generator，一个以数据为中心的框架，能够将自然语言描述的SVA（SVADs）转化为可执行的SVAs。该框架采用基于抽象语法树（AST）的约束注入机制，并结合自动化的监督流程，通过去重和约束校验来确保结构一致性，有效减少幻觉现象。为实现严格的评估，我们设计了一个按AST深度分层的基准测试套件，并利用形式化属性等价性检查方法，在相同的时钟和环境假设下，通过相互蕴含关系来分别量化语义正确性与语法有效性。在所有难度层级上，SVA Generator在语法通过率（SPR）方面与强大的通用LLM基线相当，而在深层结构（D2、D3、D4）上的语义等价率（SER）则显著更高：相较于表现最佳的通用LLM，D2提升24.5个百分点，D3提升26.0个百分点，D4提升17.5个百分点，整体在D2至D4层级上平均提升达22.7个百分点。这些结果表明，高质量数据的构建以及按深度分层的基准测试是实现可靠、语义保持的SVA生成的关键。

title: Introspective Diffusion Language Models
authors: Yifan Yu, Yuqing Jian, Junxiong Wang, Zhongzhu Zhou, Donglin Zhuang, Xinyu Fang, Sri Yanamandra, Xiaoxia Wu, Qingyang Wu, Shuaiwen Leon Song, Tri Dao, Ben Athiwaratkun, James Zou, Fan Lai, Chenfeng Xu
abstract: Diffusion language models promise parallel generation, yet still lag behind autoregressive (AR) models in quality. We stem this gap to a failure of introspective consistency: AR models agree with their own generations, while DLMs often do not. We define the introspective acceptance rate, which measures whether a model accepts its previously generated tokens. This reveals why AR training has a structural advantage: causal masking and logit shifting implicitly enforce introspective consistency. Motivated by this observation, we introduce Introspective Diffusion Language Model (I-DLM), a paradigm that retains diffusion-style parallel decoding while inheriting the introspective consistency of AR training. I-DLM uses a novel introspective strided decoding (ISD) algorithm, which enables the model to verify previously generated tokens while advancing new ones in the same forward pass. From a systems standpoint, we build I-DLM inference engine on AR-inherited optimizations and further customize it with a stationary-batch scheduler. To the best of our knowledge, I-DLM is the first DLM to match the quality of its same-scale AR counterpart while outperforming prior DLMs in both model quality and practical serving efficiency across 15 benchmarks. It reaches 69.6 on AIME-24 and 45.7 on LiveCodeBench-v6, exceeding LLaDA-2.1-mini (16B) by more than 26 and 15 points, respectively. Beyond quality, I-DLM is designed for the growing demand of large-concurrency serving, delivering about 3x higher throughput than prior state-of-the-art DLMs.
title_zh: 内省扩散语言模型
abstract_zh:
扩散语言模型（Diffusion Language Models, DLMs）承诺并行生成，但在生成质量上仍落后于自回归（Autoregressive, AR）模型。我们发现这一差距的根源在于缺乏自我一致性：AR模型能够与其自身生成的内容保持一致，而DLMs则常常做不到。为此，我们提出了“自我一致性接受率”（introspective acceptance rate），用于衡量模型是否接受其先前生成的标记。这一指标揭示了AR训练在结构上的优势：因果掩码（causal masking）和对数概率偏移（logit shifting）隐式地强制实现了自我一致性。

受此启发，我们提出了一种新的范式——**自我反思扩散语言模型**（Introspective Diffusion Language Model, I-DLM），该模型在保留扩散风格并行解码的同时，继承了AR训练中的自我一致性特性。I-DLM采用一种新颖的**自我反思步进解码算法**（Introspective Strided Decoding, ISD），使模型能够在同一前向传播过程中验证已生成的标记，并同时推进新标记的生成。

从系统实现角度看，我们基于AR模型所继承的优化技术构建了I-DLM推理引擎，并进一步通过引入**静态批次调度器**（stationary-batch scheduler）进行定制化改进。据我们所知，I-DLM是首个在相同规模下达到与AR模型相当生成质量的DLM，且在15个基准测试中，无论是模型质量还是实际服务效率，均显著优于以往的DLMs。在AIME-24上取得69.6分，在LiveCodeBench-v6上达到45.7分，分别超过LLaDA-2.1-mini（16B）模型26分和15分以上。除了卓越的质量表现，I-DLM还专为日益增长的大并发服务需求而设计，其吞吐量相比之前最先进的DLM高出约3倍。

title: Towards Automated Pentesting with Large Language Models
authors: Ricardo Bessa, Rui Claro, João Trindade, João Lourenço
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are redefining offensive cybersecurity by allowing the generation of harmful machine code with minimal human intervention. While attackers take advantage of dark LLMs such as XXXGPT and WolfGPT to produce malicious code, ethical hackers can follow similar approaches to automate traditional pentesting workflows. In this work, we present RedShell, a privacy-preserving, hardware-efficient framework that leverages fine-tuned LLMs to assist pentesters in generating offensive PowerShell code targeting Microsoft Windows vulnerabilities. RedShell was trained on a malicious PowerShell dataset from the literature, which we further enhanced with manually curated code samples. Experiments show that our framework achieves over 90% syntactic validity in generated samples and strong semantic alignment with reference pentesting snippets, outperforming state-of-the-art counterparts in distance metrics such as edit distance (above 50% average code similarity). Additionally, functional experiments emphasize the execution reliability of the snippets produced by RedShell in a testing scenario that mirrors real-world settings. This work sheds light on the state-of-the-art research in the field of Generative AI applied to malicious code generation and automated testing, acknowledging the potential benefits that LLMs hold within controlled environments such as pentesting.
title_zh: 基于大型语言模型的自动化渗透测试
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正在重新定义进攻性网络安全，使得在极少人工干预的情况下生成有害机器代码成为可能。尽管攻击者利用诸如XXXGPT和WolfGPT等暗网LLM来生成恶意代码，但道德黑客也可以采用类似方法，自动化传统的渗透测试工作流程。本文提出RedShell，这是一个注重隐私保护且硬件效率高的框架，通过微调后的LLM辅助渗透测试人员生成针对微软Windows漏洞的进攻性PowerShell代码。RedShell基于文献中已有的恶意PowerShell数据集进行训练，并进一步通过人工精心筛选的代码样本进行了扩充。实验结果表明，该框架生成的代码样本在语法正确性方面超过90%，且在语义上与参考渗透测试片段高度一致，在编辑距离等距离度量指标上表现优于当前最先进的方法（平均代码相似度提升超过50%）。此外，功能实验也验证了RedShell生成代码片段在模拟真实世界环境的测试场景中的执行可靠性。本研究揭示了生成式人工智能应用于恶意代码生成与自动化测试领域的最新进展，同时承认LLM在受控环境（如渗透测试）中所蕴含的巨大潜力。

title: Utilizing and Calibrating Hindsight Process Rewards via Reinforcement with Mutual Information Self-Evaluation
authors: Jiashu Yao, Heyan Huang, Zeming Liu, Yuhang Guo
abstract: To overcome the sparse reward challenge in reinforcement learning (RL) for agents based on large language models (LLMs), we propose Mutual Information Self-Evaluation (MISE), an RL paradigm that utilizes hindsight generative self-evaluation as dense reward signals while simultaneously calibrating them against the environmental feedbacks. Empirically, MISE enables an agent to learn autonomously from dense internal rewards supplementing sparse extrinsic signals. Theoretically, our work provides the first formal foundation for the paradigm of generative self-rewarding. We prove that utilizing hindsight self-evaluation rewards is equivalent to minimizing an objective that combines mutual information with a KL divergence term between the policy and a proxy reward policy. This theoretical insight then informs and justifies our calibration step, which actively aligns these rewards with the optimal policy. Extensive experiments show that MISE outperforms strong baselines, enabling open-source LLMs about 7B parameters to achieve performance comparable to GPT-4o on validation without expert supervision.
title_zh: 通过互信息自评估进行强化学习的后见过程奖励利用与校准
abstract_zh: 为克服基于大语言模型（LLMs）的智能体在强化学习（RL）中面临的稀疏奖励挑战，我们提出了一种名为互信息自评估（Mutual Information Self-Evaluation, MISE）的强化学习范式。该方法利用事后生成的自评估作为密集奖励信号，同时将这些信号与环境反馈进行校准。实验结果表明，MISE使智能体能够从密集的内部奖励中自主学习，从而补充稀疏的外部信号。理论上，我们的工作首次为生成式自奖励范式提供了正式基础。我们证明，使用事后自评估奖励等价于最小化一个结合了互信息与策略和代理奖励策略之间KL散度的优化目标。这一理论洞察为我们的校准步骤提供了依据，该步骤主动将奖励与最优策略对齐。大量实验表明，MISE显著优于多个强基线方法，使得参数量约为70亿的开源LLM在无需专家监督的情况下，其性能可达到GPT-4o在验证集上的水平。

title: From Translation to Superset: Benchmark-Driven Evolution of a Production AI Agent from Rust to Python
authors: Jinhua Wang, Biswa Sengupta
abstract: Cross-language migration of large software systems is a persistent engineering challenge, particularly when the source codebase evolves rapidly. We present a methodology for LLM-assisted continuous code translation in which a large language model translates a production Rust codebase (648K LOC, 65 crates) into Python (41K LOC, 28 modules), with public agent benchmarks as the objective function driving iterative refinement. Our subject system is Codex CLI, a production AI coding agent. We demonstrate that: (1) the Python port resolves 59/80 SWE-bench Verified tasks (73.8%) versus Rust's 56/80 (70.0%), and achieves 42.5% on Terminal-Bench versus Rust's 47.5%, confirming near-parity on real-world agentic tasks; (2) benchmark-driven debugging, revealing API protocol mismatches, environment pollution, a silent WebSocket failure mode, and an API 400 crash, is more effective than static testing alone; (3) the architecture supports continuous upstream synchronisation via an LLM-assisted diff-translate-test loop; and (4) the Python port has evolved into a capability superset with 30 feature-flagged extensions (multi-agent orchestration, semantic memory, guardian safety, cost tracking) absent from Rust, while preserving strict parity mode for comparison. Our evaluation shows that for LLM-based agents where API latency dominates, Python's expressiveness yields a 15.9x code reduction with negligible performance cost, while the benchmark-as-objective-function methodology provides a principled framework for growing a cross-language port from parity into an extended platform.
title_zh: 从翻译到超集：基于基准的生产级AI代理从Rust到Python的演进
abstract_zh: 跨语言大型软件系统的迁移是一个持续存在的工程挑战，尤其是在源代码库快速演进的情况下。我们提出了一种由大语言模型（LLM）辅助的持续代码翻译方法：将一个生产级的 Rust 代码库（64.8 万行代码，65 个 crate）翻译为 Python（4.1 万行代码，28 个模块），并以公开的代理基准测试作为目标函数，驱动迭代优化。我们的研究对象是 Codex CLI——一款生产环境中的 AI 编程代理系统。实验结果表明：（1）Python 版本在 SWE-bench Verified 任务中成功解决 59/80 项（73.8%），优于 Rust 版本的 56/80（70.0%）；在 Terminal-Bench 基准上，Python 实现 42.5% 的通过率，略低于 Rust 的 47.5%，证实了在真实世界代理任务中两者表现接近；（2）基于基准测试的调试机制揭示了 API 协议不匹配、环境污染、无声 WebSocket 失效模式以及 API 400 错误崩溃等问题，其效果显著优于仅依赖静态测试的方法；（3）该架构支持通过 LLM 辅助的 diff-translate-test 循环实现持续的上游同步；（4）Python 版本已发展为功能超集，新增了 30 个受功能标志控制的扩展特性（包括多代理编排、语义记忆、安全守护、成本追踪等），这些功能在原始 Rust 版本中并不存在，同时仍保留严格对齐模式以供对比。评估结果表明，在以 API 延迟为主导的 LLM 代理系统中，Python 凭借更高的表达能力实现了 15.9 倍的代码量缩减，且性能损耗可忽略不计；而“以基准测试为目标函数”的方法论为从语言间功能对齐逐步演进为功能增强平台提供了严谨的框架。

title: DuET: Dual Execution for Test Output Prediction with Generated Code and Pseudocode
authors: Hojae Han, Jaejin Kim, Seung-won Hwang, Yu Jin Kim, Moontae Lee
abstract: This work addresses test output prediction, a key challenge in test case generation. To improve the reliability of predicted outputs by LLMs, prior approaches generate code first to ground predictions. One grounding strategy is direct execution of generated code, but even minor errors can cause failures. To address this, we introduce LLM-based pseudocode execution, which grounds prediction on more error-resilient pseudocode and simulates execution via LLM reasoning. We further propose DuET, a dual-execution framework that combines both approaches by functional majority voting. Our analysis shows the two approaches are complementary in overcoming the limitations of direct execution suffering from code errors, and pseudocode reasoning from hallucination. On LiveCodeBench, DuET achieves the state-of-the-art performance, improving Pass@1 by 13.6 pp.
title_zh: DuET：基于生成代码与伪代码的测试输出预测双重执行方法
abstract_zh: 本文针对测试用例生成中的测试输出预测这一关键挑战展开研究。为提升大语言模型（LLM）预测输出的可靠性，现有方法通常先生成代码以实现预测的“锚定”。一种常见的锚定策略是直接执行生成的代码，但即使存在微小错误也可能导致失败。为此，我们提出基于LLM的伪代码执行方法，通过更容错的伪代码来锚定预测，并利用LLM推理模拟执行过程。进一步地，我们提出了DuET——一种双执行框架，通过功能多数投票机制融合上述两种方法。分析表明，这两种方法在克服直接执行因代码错误导致的失败以及伪代码推理中幻觉问题方面具有互补性。在LiveCodeBench基准上，DuET达到了当前最优性能，Pass@1指标提升了13.6个百分点。

title: Policy Split: Incentivizing Dual-Mode Exploration in LLM Reinforcement with Dual-Mode Entropy Regularization
authors: Jiashu Yao, Heyan Huang, Chuwei Luo, Daiqing Wu, Zeming Liu, Yuhang Guo, Yangyang Kang
abstract: To encourage diverse exploration in reinforcement learning (RL) for large language models (LLMs) without compromising accuracy, we propose Policy Split, a novel paradigm that bifurcates the policy into normal and high-entropy modes with a high-entropy prompt. While sharing model parameters, the two modes undergo collaborative dual-mode entropy regularization tailored to distinct objectives. Specifically, the normal mode optimizes for task correctness, while the high-entropy mode incorporates a preference for exploration, and the two modes learn collaboratively. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach consistently outperforms established entropy-guided RL baselines across various model sizes in general and creative tasks. Further analysis reveals that Policy Split facilitates dual-mode exploration, where the high-entropy mode generates distinct behavioral patterns to the normal mode, providing unique learning signals.
title_zh: 策略拆分：基于双模式熵正则化的大型语言模型强化学习中的双重模式探索激励
abstract_zh: 为在不牺牲准确性的前提下，鼓励大规模语言模型（LLMs）在强化学习（RL）中进行多样化探索，我们提出了Policy Split这一新范式。该方法通过一个高熵提示，将策略分为正常模式与高熵模式两种。尽管共享模型参数，这两种模式仍针对不同目标，实施协同的双模式熵正则化。具体而言，正常模式优化任务正确性，而高熵模式则倾向于探索，并与正常模式协同学习。大量实验表明，我们的方法在各类模型规模下，于通用任务和创造性任务中均持续优于现有的熵引导强化学习基线。进一步分析显示，Policy Split实现了双模式探索：高熵模式生成的行为模式与正常模式显著不同，从而提供了独特的学习信号。

title: RedShell: A Generative AI-Based Approach to Ethical Hacking
authors: Ricardo Bessa, Rui Claro, João Trindade, João Lourenço
abstract: The application of Machine Learning techniques in code generation is now a common practice for most developers. Tools such as ChatGPT from OpenAI leverage the natural language processing capabilities of Large Language Models to generate machine code from natural language descriptions. In the cybersecurity field, red teams can also take advantage of generative models to build malicious code generators, providing more automation to Pentest audits. However, the application of Large Language Models in malicious code generation remains challenging due to the lack of data to train and evaluate offensive code generators. In this work, we propose RedShell, a tool that allows ethical hackers to generate malicious PowerShell code. We also introduce a ground truth dataset, combining publicly available code samples to fine-tune models in malicious PowerShell generation. Our experiments demonstrate the strong capabilities of RedShell in generating syntactically valid PowerShell, with fewer than 10% of the generated samples resulting in parse errors. Furthermore, our specialized model was able to produce samples that were semantically consistent with reference snippets, achieving a competitive performance on standard output similarity metrics such as Edit Distance and METEOR, with their mean similarity scores exceeding 50% and 40%, respectively. This work sheds light on the state-of-the-art research in the field of Generative AI applied to Pentesting, and also serves as a steppingstone for future advancements, highlighting the potential benefits these models hold within such controlled environments.
title_zh: RedShell：一种基于生成式人工智能的伦理黑客技术
abstract_zh: 机器学习技术在代码生成领域的应用如今已成为大多数开发者的常规实践。像OpenAI推出的ChatGPT这类工具，利用大型语言模型的自然语言处理能力，能够根据自然语言描述生成机器代码。在网络安全领域，红队人员同样可以借助生成式模型构建恶意代码生成器，从而提升渗透测试审计的自动化水平。然而，由于缺乏用于训练和评估进攻性代码生成器的数据，大型语言模型在恶意代码生成中的应用仍面临诸多挑战。本文提出了一款名为RedShell的工具，使道德黑客能够生成恶意PowerShell代码。我们还构建了一个基准数据集，整合了公开可获取的代码样本，用于对模型进行微调，以提升其在恶意PowerShell代码生成方面的性能。实验结果表明，RedShell在生成语法正确的PowerShell代码方面表现出色，生成样本中解析错误率低于10%。此外，我们专门优化的模型所生成的代码在语义上与参考片段保持高度一致，在标准输出相似性度量（如编辑距离和METEOR）上表现优异，其平均相似度得分分别超过50%和40%。本研究揭示了生成式人工智能应用于渗透测试领域的最新进展，也为未来的技术演进奠定了基础，凸显了此类模型在受控环境中的巨大潜力。

title: Retrieval as Generation: A Unified Framework with Self-Triggered Information Planning
authors: Bo Li, Mingda Wang, Gexiang Fang, Shikun Zhang, Wei Ye
abstract: We revisit retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) by embedding retrieval control directly into generation. Instead of treating retrieval as an external intervention, we express retrieval decisions within token-level decoding, enabling end-to-end coordination without additional controllers or classifiers. Under the paradigm of Retrieval as Generation, we propose \textbf{GRIP} (\textbf{G}eneration-guided \textbf{R}etrieval with \textbf{I}nformation \textbf{P}lanning), a unified framework in which the model regulates retrieval behavior through control-token emission. Central to GRIP is \textit{Self-Triggered Information Planning}, which allows the model to decide when to retrieve, how to reformulate queries, and when to terminate, all within a single autoregressive trajectory. This design tightly couples retrieval and reasoning and supports dynamic multi-step inference with on-the-fly evidence integration. To supervise these behaviors, we construct a structured training set covering answerable, partially answerable, and multi-hop queries, each aligned with specific token patterns. Experiments on five QA benchmarks show that GRIP surpasses strong RAG baselines and is competitive with GPT-4o while using substantially fewer parameters.
title_zh: 检索即生成：一种具有自触发信息规划的统一框架
abstract_zh: 我们重新审视了检索增强生成（RAG），将检索控制直接嵌入生成过程。与以往将检索视为外部干预的做法不同，我们通过在词元级解码中表达检索决策，实现了无需额外控制器或分类器的端到端协同。在“检索即生成”的范式下，我们提出了**GRIP**（**G**eneration-guided **R**etrieval with **I**nformation **P**lanning），这是一个统一框架，模型通过发出控制标记来调节检索行为。GRIP的核心是**自触发信息规划**，使模型能够在单一自回归轨迹中自主决定何时进行检索、如何重构查询以及何时终止，从而紧密耦合检索与推理，并支持动态的多步推理及实时证据整合。为监督这些行为，我们构建了一个结构化训练数据集，涵盖可回答、部分可回答和多跳查询，每类查询均对应特定的词元模式。在五个问答基准上的实验表明，GRIP超越了现有的强大RAG基线模型，在参数量远少于GPT-4o的情况下，性能仍具有竞争力。

title: Learning from Contrasts: Synthesizing Reasoning Paths from Diverse Search Trajectories
authors: Peiyang Liu, Zhirui Chen, Xi Wang, Di Liang, Youru Li, Zhi Cai, Wei Ye
abstract: Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) has been widely used for automated reasoning data exploration, but current supervision extraction methods remain inefficient. Standard approaches retain only the single highest-reward trajectory, discarding the comparative signals present in the many explored paths. Here we introduce \textbf{Contrastive Reasoning Path Synthesis (CRPS)}, a framework that transforms supervision extraction from a filtering process into a synthesis procedure. CRPS uses a structured reflective process to analyze the differences between high- and low-quality search trajectories, extracting explicit information about strategic pivots and local failure modes. These insights guide the synthesis of reasoning chains that incorporate success patterns while avoiding identified pitfalls. We show empirically that models fine-tuned on just 60K CRPS-synthesized examples match or exceed the performance of baselines trained on 590K examples derived from standard rejection sampling, a 20$\times$ reduction in dataset size. Furthermore, CRPS improves generalization on out-of-domain benchmarks, demonstrating that learning from the contrast between success and failure produces more transferable reasoning capabilities than learning from success alone.
title_zh: 从对比中学习：整合多样化搜索轨迹的推理路径
abstract_zh: 蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）已被广泛应用于自动化推理数据探索，但现有的监督信号提取方法仍效率低下。传统方法仅保留单一最高奖励的轨迹，忽略了大量探索路径中蕴含的对比性信息。本文提出一种新框架——**对比推理路径合成（Contrastive Reasoning Path Synthesis, CRPS）**，将监督信号提取从传统的筛选过程转变为一种合成过程。CRPS通过结构化的反思机制，分析高质量与低质量搜索轨迹之间的差异，从而提取关于策略转折点和局部失败模式的明确信息。这些洞察指导生成新的推理链，既融合成功模式，又规避已识别的陷阱。实验证明，仅使用6万条CRPS合成样本进行微调的模型，在性能上即可达到甚至超过基于标准拒绝采样生成的59万条样本训练的基线模型，实现数据集规模20倍的缩减。此外，CRPS在跨领域基准测试中展现出更强的泛化能力，表明从成功与失败的对比中学习，能够产生比仅从成功案例中学习更具备迁移性的推理能力。

title: Ro-SLM: Onboard Small Language Models for Robot Task Planning and Operation Code Generation
authors: Wenhao Wang, Yanyan Li, Long Jiao, Jiawei Yuan
abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) provide robots with contextual reasoning abilities to comprehend human instructions. Yet, current LLM-enabled robots typically depend on cloud-based models or high-performance computing infrastructure, which limit their deployment on robots under unreliable internet environments or with constrained computational resources, such as UAVs and small ground vehicles. Thus, deploying fine-tuned small language models (SLMs) that support onboard deployment offers a promising alternative. This paper introduces Ro-SLM, a framework that enables reliable SLM-driven robot operation by distilling LLMs' knowledge and reasoning. Ro-SLM starts from dataset synthesis by leveraging LLMs to generate diverse task instructions, produce corresponding ground truth code with minimal human assistance, and augment instructions into real-world application scenarios. Ro-SLM is then fine-tuned with the dataset, in which LLM serves as a reward function to guide the training. Extensive experiments on UAV operation tasks demonstrate that Ro-SLM improves the performance of SLM from being incapable of supporting robotic task planning and code generation to achieving performance that approaches LLM.
title_zh: Ro-SLM：用于机器人任务规划与操作代码生成的机载小型语言模型
abstract_zh: 近年来，大型语言模型（LLMs）的进展为机器人提供了上下文推理能力，使其能够理解人类指令。然而，当前基于LLM的机器人通常依赖于云端模型或高性能计算基础设施，这限制了其在互联网环境不稳定或计算资源受限的机器人上的部署，例如无人机（UAV）和小型地面车辆。因此，部署经过微调的小型语言模型（SLMs）以支持机载运行，提供了一种有前景的替代方案。本文提出Ro-SLM框架，通过从LLMs中提炼知识与推理能力，实现可靠的小型语言模型驱动的机器人操作。Ro-SLM首先利用LLMs生成多样化的任务指令，以最少的人工干预生成相应的正确代码，并将这些指令扩展至真实应用场景中，完成数据集的合成。随后，Ro-SLM使用该数据集进行微调，其中LLM作为奖励函数指导训练过程。在无人机操作任务上的大量实验表明，Ro-SLM成功将SLM的性能从无法支持机器人任务规划与代码生成，提升至接近LLM的水平。

title: OccuBench: Evaluating AI Agents on Real-World Professional Tasks via Language World Models
authors: Xiaomeng Hu, Yinger Zhang, Fei Huang, Jianhong Tu, Yang Su, Lianghao Deng, Yuxuan Liu, Yantao Liu, Dayiheng Liu, Tsung-Yi Ho
abstract: AI agents are expected to perform professional work across hundreds of occupational domains (from emergency department triage to nuclear reactor safety monitoring to customs import processing), yet existing benchmarks can only evaluate agents in the few domains where public environments exist. We introduce OccuBench, a benchmark covering 100 real-world professional task scenarios across 10 industry categories and 65 specialized domains, enabled by Language World Models (LWMs) that simulate domain-specific environments through LLM-driven tool response generation. Our multi-agent synthesis pipeline automatically produces evaluation instances with guaranteed solvability, calibrated difficulty, and document-grounded diversity. OccuBench evaluates agents along two complementary dimensions: task completion across professional domains and environmental robustness under controlled fault injection (explicit errors, implicit data degradation, and mixed faults). We evaluate 15 frontier models across 8 model families and find that: (1) no single model dominates all industries, as each has a distinct occupational capability profile; (2) implicit faults (truncated data, missing fields) are harder than both explicit errors (timeouts, 500s) and mixed faults, because they lack overt error signals and require the agent to independently detect data degradation; (3) larger models, newer generations, and higher reasoning effort consistently improve performance. GPT-5.2 improves by 27.5 points from minimal to maximum reasoning effort; and (4) strong agents are not necessarily strong environment simulators. Simulator quality is critical for LWM-based evaluation reliability. OccuBench provides the first systematic cross-industry evaluation of AI agents on professional occupational tasks.
title_zh: OccuBench：通过语言世界模型评估AI代理在现实世界专业任务中的表现
abstract_zh:
AI代理预计将在数百个职业领域（从急诊科分诊到核反应堆安全监控，再到海关进口处理）中执行专业工作，但现有的评估基准仅能对存在公开环境的少数领域进行评测。为此，我们推出了OccuBench——一个涵盖10个行业类别、65个专业领域共计100个真实世界职业任务场景的基准测试平台。该平台依托语言世界模型（LWMs），通过大语言模型（LLM）驱动的工具响应生成技术，模拟出各领域的专用环境。我们的多智能体合成流程可自动生成具有保证可解性、难度校准以及基于文档的多样性评估实例。

OccuBench从两个互补维度评估AI代理：一是跨职业领域的任务完成能力，二是面对受控故障注入时的环境鲁棒性（包括显式错误、隐式数据退化以及混合故障）。我们在8个模型家族中的15个前沿模型上进行了评估，发现：（1）没有单一模型在所有行业中占据绝对优势，每个模型均展现出独特的职能力量分布特征；（2）隐式故障（如数据截断、字段缺失）比显式错误（如超时、500错误）和混合故障更具挑战性，因其缺乏明显的错误信号，要求代理自主识别数据质量下降；（3）模型规模越大、版本越新、推理投入越多，性能越优；GPT-5.2在最小与最大推理努力之间性能提升达27.5分；（4）强大的代理并不等同于优秀的环境模拟器。模拟器的质量对基于LWM的评估可靠性至关重要。

OccuBench首次系统性地实现了AI代理在跨行业职业任务上的全面评估。

title: Enhancing Program Repair with Specification Guidance and Intermediate Behavioral Signals
authors: Minh Le-Anh, Cuong Chi Le, Tien N. Nguyen
abstract: Automated Program Repair (APR) has recently benefited from large language models (LLMs). However, most LLM-based APR approaches still rely primarily on coarse end-to-end signals from test-suite outcomes to guide repair, providing limited insight into where a program's internal logic deviates from its intended behavior. In contrast, human debugging often relies on intermediate reasoning about program states through localized correctness conditions or assertions. Inspired by this observation, we propose SpecTune, a specification-guided debugging framework that incorporates intermediate behavioral reasoning into APR. SpecTune decomposes the repair task into suspicious regions connected by execution checkpoints and derives localized postconditions representing expected program behaviors at those points. By executing the buggy program and evaluating these postconditions, SpecTune produces micro-level debugging signals that indicate mismatches between observed and intended behaviors, enabling more precise fault localization and targeted patch generation. To address the potential unreliability of LLM-generated postconditions, we introduce two complementary signals: a specification validation signal alpha, which estimates the consistency of generated postconditions using partially passing test cases, and a discriminative signal beta, which detects violations of validated postconditions during execution. With these signals, SpecTune safely leverages automatically generated specifications for APR. Experimental results show that SpecTune improves fault localization and APR effectiveness than the baselines.
title_zh: 利用规范指导和中间行为信号增强程序修复
abstract_zh: 自动化程序修复（APR）近年来受益于大规模语言模型（LLMs）的发展。然而，大多数基于LLM的APR方法仍然主要依赖于测试套件结果提供的粗粒度端到端信号来指导修复，难以深入揭示程序内部逻辑与预期行为之间的偏差。相比之下，人类调试通常依赖于对程序状态的中间推理，通过局部正确性条件或断言来判断程序是否按预期运行。受此启发，我们提出了SpecTune——一种基于规范引导的调试框架，将中间行为推理引入APR过程。SpecTune将修复任务分解为由执行检查点连接的可疑区域，并推导出这些关键点处所期望程序行为的局部后置条件。通过执行存在缺陷的程序并评估这些后置条件，SpecTune生成细粒度的调试信号，用以指示观察到的行为与预期行为之间的不一致，从而实现更精确的故障定位和针对性的补丁生成。为应对LLM生成后置条件可能存在的不可靠性问题，我们引入了两种互补的信号：一是规范验证信号α，利用部分通过的测试用例估计生成后置条件的一致性；二是判别信号β，用于在执行过程中检测已验证后置条件的违反情况。借助这两种信号，SpecTune能够安全地利用自动生成的规范进行APR。实验结果表明，与基线方法相比，SpecTune显著提升了故障定位精度和APR的整体有效性。

title: E2E-REME: Towards End-to-End Microservices Auto-Remediation via Experience-Simulation Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
authors: Lingzhe Zhang, Yunpeng Zhai, Tong Jia, Minghua He, Chiming Duan, Zhaoyang Liu, Bolin Ding, Ying Li
abstract: Contemporary microservice systems continue to grow in scale and complexity, leading to increasingly frequent and costly failures. While recent LLM-based auto-remediation approaches have emerged, they primarily translate textual instructions into executable Ansible playbooks and rely on expert-crafted prompts, lacking runtime knowledge guidance and depending on large-scale general-purpose LLMs, which limits their accuracy and efficiency. We introduce \textit{End-to-End Microservice Remediation} (E2E-MR), a new task that requires directly generating executable playbooks from diagnosis reports to autonomously restore faulty systems. To enable rigorous evaluation, we build \textit{MicroRemed}, a benchmark that automates microservice deployment, failure injection, playbook execution, and post-repair verification. We further propose \textit{E2E-REME}, an end-to-end auto-remediation model trained via experience-simulation reinforcement fine-tuning. Experiments on public and industrial microservice platforms, compared with nine representative LLMs, show that E2E-REME achieves superior accuracy and efficiency.
title_zh: E2E-REME：通过经验-仿真强化微调实现端到端微服务自动修复
abstract_zh: 当代微服务系统规模与复杂性持续增长，导致故障频发且修复成本不断上升。尽管近期出现了基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动修复方法，但这些方法主要将文本指令转换为可执行的 Ansible 任务剧本，并依赖专家手工设计的提示词（prompts），缺乏运行时知识引导，且严重依赖大规模通用大语言模型，因而限制了其准确性和效率。为此，我们提出了 \textit{端到端微服务修复}（E2E-MR）这一新任务，旨在直接从诊断报告生成可执行的修复剧本，以实现故障系统的自主恢复。为支持严谨的评估，我们构建了 \textit{MicroRemed} 基准测试平台，该平台自动化完成微服务部署、故障注入、剧本执行及修复后验证等全流程。此外，我们进一步提出 \textit{E2E-REME} 模型，一种通过经验-仿真强化学习微调训练的端到端自动修复模型。在公开和工业级微服务平台上的实验结果表明，相较于九种代表性 LLM 方法，E2E-REME 在准确率和效率方面均表现出显著优势。

title: Fast Atomicity Monitoring
authors: Hünkar Can Tun, Yifan Dong, Andreas Pavlogiannis
abstract: Atomicity is a fundamental abstraction in concurrency, specifying that program behavior can be understood by considering specific code blocks executing atomically. However, atomicity invariants are tricky to maintain while also optimizing for code efficiency, and atomicity violations are a common root cause of many concurrency bugs. To address this problem, several dynamic techniques have been developed for testing whether a program execution adheres to an atomicity specification, most often instantiated as \emph{conflict serializability}. The efficiency of the analysis has been targeted in various papers, with the state-of-the-art algorithms \textsc{RegionTrack} and \textsc{Aerodrome} achieving a time complexity $O(nk^3)$ and $O(nk(k + v + \ell))$, respectively, for a trace $σ$ of $n$ events, $k$ threads, $v$ locations, and $\ell$ locks. In this paper we introduce \textsc{AtomSanitizer}, a new algorithm for testing conflict serializability, with time complexity $O(nk^2)$. \textsc{AtomSanitizer} operates in an efficient streaming style, is theoretically faster than all existing algorithms, and also has a smaller memory footprint. Moreover, \textsc{AtomSanitizer} is the first algorithm designed to incur minimal locking when deployed in a concurrent monitoring setting. Experiments on standard benchmarks indicate that \textsc{AtomSanitizer} is always faster in practice than all existing conflict-serializability testers. Finally, we also implement \textsc{AtomSanitizer} inside the TSAN framework, for monitoring atomicity in real time. Our experiments reveal that \textsc{AtomSanitizer} incurs minimal time and space overhead compared to the data-race detection engine of TSAN, and thus is the first algorithm for conflict serializability demonstrated to be suitable for a runtime monitoring setting.
title_zh: 快速原子性监控
abstract_zh:
原子性是并发编程中的一个基本抽象，它规定程序的行为可以通过考虑某些代码块以原子方式执行来理解。然而，在保持原子性不变量的同时实现代码效率优化却十分困难，而原子性违规往往是许多并发错误的根本原因。为解决这一问题，研究人员开发了多种动态技术，用于测试程序执行是否符合原子性规范，通常以**冲突可串行化**（conflict serializability）的形式体现。已有研究致力于提升分析效率，其中最先进的算法——\textsc{RegionTrack} 和 \textsc{Aerodrome}——的时间复杂度分别为 $O(nk^3)$ 和 $O(nk(k + v + \ell))$，分别对应于包含 $n$ 个事件、$k$ 个线程、$v$ 个内存位置和 $\ell$ 个锁的执行轨迹 $σ$。

本文提出了一种名为 \textsc{AtomSanitizer} 的新算法，用于检测冲突可串行性，其时间复杂度为 $O(nk^2)$。该算法采用高效的流式处理模式，理论上比所有现有算法更快，并且具有更小的内存开销。此外，\textsc{AtomSanitizer} 是首个专为在并发监控环境中部署时尽量减少锁开销而设计的算法。在标准基准测试上的实验表明，\textsc{AtomSanitizer} 在实际运行中始终优于所有现有的冲突可串行性检测工具。

最后，我们将 \textsc{AtomSanitizer} 集成到 TSAN 框架中，实现了对原子性的实时监控。实验结果表明，与 TSAN 的数据竞争检测引擎相比，\textsc{AtomSanitizer} 所带来的时间和空间开销极小，因此它是首个被证明适用于运行时监控场景的冲突可串行性算法。

title: Foundations of the GraphAlg Language
authors: Daan de Graaf, Robert Brijder, Nikolay Yakovets
abstract: The GraphAlg domain-specific language for graph algorithms enables user-defined algorithms in graph databases. In this work we show how GraphAlg is built on top of the formal MATLANG language for matrix manipulation. Starting from MATLANG, we describe the extensions to MATLANG needed to derive GraphAlg Core, a simplified version of GraphAlg that is used as the internal representation in the GraphAlg compiler. Furthermore, we prove that any GraphAlg Core expression can be simulated in an extension of for-MATLANG that supports simultaneous induction.
title_zh: GraphAlg语言的基础
abstract_zh: 图算法领域特定语言（GraphAlg）允许用户在图数据库中定义自己的图算法。本文展示了GraphAlg如何基于用于矩阵操作的正式语言MATLANG构建而成。从MATLANG出发，我们描述了为推导出GraphAlg核心（GraphAlg Core）所需对MATLANG进行的扩展，GraphAlg核心是GraphAlg的一个简化版本，用作GraphAlg编译器中的内部表示形式。此外，我们证明了任何GraphAlg核心表达式都可以在一种支持同时归纳的MATLANG扩展中被模拟。

title: Leveraging Generative AI for Security Defense of Mobile Edge-Enabled Digital Twins: A Survey
authors: Yaoqi Yang, Geng Sun, Hongyang Du, Changyuan Zhao, Zehui Xiong, Dusit Niyato, Abbas Jamalipour, Zhu Han, Khaled B. Letaief
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: Addressing rising security threats that hinder the deployment of mobile edge-enabled digital twins (MDTs), this paper investigates the transformative potential of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) for enhancing MDT security frameworks. We propose a comprehensive five-layer MDT architecture and analyze various malicious security threats across physical and digital twin spaces. Focusing on GAI’s unique capabilities in data quality and diversity, we explore the application of variational autoencoders, generative adversarial networks, generated diffusion models, and transformer-based models within different layers of the MDT architecture. We also provide a summary of performance metrics, evaluation methodologies, and implementation considerations, concluding with key future research directions aimed at realizing real-time and complex attack defense strategies. Our findings show that GAI can significantly improve security measures for MDT systems, effectively addressing critical challenges in this rapidly evolving field.
title_zh: 利用生成式人工智能增强移动边缘赋能数字孪生的安全防护：一项综述
abstract_zh: 针对阻碍移动边缘赋能数字孪生（MDT）部署的日益严峻的安全威胁，本文探讨了生成式人工智能（GAI）在提升MDT安全框架方面的变革潜力。我们提出了一种全面的五层MDT架构，并分析了物理空间与数字孪生空间中各类恶意安全威胁。聚焦GAI在数据质量与多样性方面的独特能力，本文研究了变分自编码器、生成对抗网络、生成扩散模型以及基于Transformer的模型在MDT架构不同层级中的应用。同时，本文总结了性能指标、评估方法及实施考量，并展望了未来关键的研究方向，旨在实现实时且复杂的攻击防御策略。研究结果表明，GAI能够显著增强MDT系统的安全防护能力，有效应对该快速演进领域中的关键挑战。

title: Demonstrating Code Mutations Using LLMs: A Sandbox Approach to Safe Malware Testing
authors: Indhuja. E, Noel Ashik.J.S, Dheeraj. K, Surya Prakash.V
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: There is a rapid increase in the use of Large Language Models (LLMs). These models have made advanced code generators democratically available, introducing a dual-use threat where automated agents can be directed to iteratively generate malware. Although existing literature discusses static code obfuscation, few studies investigate the effectiveness of closed-loop execution feedback in evading contemporary behavioral defenses. This paper proposes an autonomous malware mutation framework utilizing an uncensored local LLM (Dolphin 3.0) to produce, execute, and debug malicious payloads within an instrumented Windows 10 sandbox. In contrast to conventional one-shot generation techniques, this system adopts a genetic evolution cycle, using runtime error logs and Antivirus (AV) detection events to guide the LLM in rewriting code. The framework was evaluated in a two-phase longitudinal study against Windows Defender with Real-Time Protection fully enabled and no folder exclusions. Phase I (Exploratory) established a baseline over 14 random runs, where ‘noisy’ ransomware activities were successfully identified by Controlled Folder Access (28.6% detection rate). Phase II (Targeted Evasion), however, refined payloads across four distinct categories of malware-Reconnaissance, Espionage, Command and Control and Sabotage- 100 percent evasion. These results reveal a lethal loophole in currently deployed behavioral heuristics in that they could not identify malicious behavior on Python-based automatically-executed executables, even in the midst of aggressive actions like network tunneling and file encryption attempts. The findings explain that despite the effectiveness of commercial AVs in terms of known signatures, they are susceptible to the polymorphism and iterativeness of code generated by LLMs.
title_zh: 使用大语言模型展示代码变异：一种安全的恶意软件测试沙箱方法
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的使用正迅速增长。这些模型已使先进的代码生成工具变得普及，从而引入了双重用途风险：自动化代理可被引导以迭代方式生成恶意软件。尽管现有文献讨论了静态代码混淆技术，但很少有研究探讨闭环执行反馈在规避现代行为防御机制方面的有效性。本文提出了一种自主的恶意软件变异框架，利用未受限制的本地大语言模型（Dolphin 3.0），在经过仪器化的Windows 10沙箱环境中实现恶意载荷的生成、执行与调试。与传统的单次生成技术不同，该系统采用遗传进化循环机制，通过运行时错误日志和杀毒软件（AV）检测事件来指导LLM重写代码。该框架在两阶段纵向研究中进行了评估，测试环境为启用实时保护且无文件夹排除项的Windows Defender。第一阶段（探索性阶段）在14次随机运行中建立了基线，成功通过受控文件夹访问功能识别出“嘈杂”的勒索软件活动（检测率为28.6%）。第二阶段（针对性规避阶段）则针对四类不同恶意软件——侦察、间谍活动、命令与控制以及破坏行为——对载荷进行了优化，实现了100%的规避效果。研究结果揭示了当前部署的行为启发式防御体系存在致命漏洞：它们无法识别基于Python自执行可执行文件中的恶意行为，即使在进行如网络隧道和文件加密等激进操作时亦然。这一发现表明，尽管商业杀毒软件在已知特征匹配方面表现有效，但面对由LLM生成的具有多态性和迭代性的代码时，仍显得极为脆弱。

title: Large-Scale Empirical Analysis of Continuous Fuzzing: Insights from 1 Million Fuzzing Sessions
authors: Tatsuya Shirai, Olivier Nourry, Yutaro Kashiwa, Kenji Fujiwara, Yasutaka Kamei, Hajimu Iida
abstract:
Software vulnerabilities are constantly being reported and exploited in software products, causing significant impacts on society. In recent years, the main approach to vulnerability detection, fuzzing, has been integrated into the continuous integration process to run in short and frequent cycles. This continuous fuzzing allows for fast identification and remediation of vulnerabilities during the development process. Despite adoption by thousands of projects, however, it is unclear how continuous fuzzing contributes to vulnerability detection.

This study aims to elucidate the role of continuous fuzzing in vulnerability detection. Specifically, we investigate the coverage and the total number of fuzzing sessions when fuzzing bugs are discovered. We collect issue reports, coverage reports, and fuzzing logs from OSS-Fuzz, an online service provided by Google that performs fuzzing during continuous integration. Through an empirical study of a total of approximately 1.12 million fuzzing sessions from 878 projects participating in OSS-Fuzz, we reveal that (i) a substantial number of fuzzing bugs exist prior to the integration of continuous fuzzing, leading to a high detection rate in the early stages; (ii) code coverage continues to increase as continuous fuzzing progresses; (iii) changes in coverage contribute to the detection of fuzzing bugs; and (iv) developer-provided seed corpus exhibit long-term effectiveness. This study provides empirical insights into how continuous fuzzing contributes to fuzzing bug detection, offering practical implications for future strategies and tool development in continuous fuzzing.
title_zh: 大规模连续模糊测试的实证分析：来自一百万次模糊测试会话的洞察
abstract_zh:
软件漏洞不断被报告并被利用于各类软件产品中，对社会造成了重大影响。近年来，主要的漏洞检测方法——模糊测试（fuzzing）已被集成到持续集成流程中，以短周期、高频次的方式运行。这种持续模糊测试能够在开发过程中快速发现并修复漏洞。然而，尽管已有数千个开源项目采用该方法，但持续模糊测试在漏洞检测中的具体作用仍不明确。

本研究旨在阐明持续模糊测试在漏洞检测中的作用。具体而言，我们考察了在发现模糊测试漏洞时的代码覆盖率以及模糊测试会话的总次数。我们从Google提供的在线服务OSS-Fuzz中收集了问题报告、覆盖率报告和模糊测试日志。通过对参与OSS-Fuzz的878个项目的约112万次模糊测试会话进行实证研究，我们发现：（i）在引入持续模糊测试之前，已存在大量模糊测试漏洞，导致早期阶段的漏洞检测率较高；（ii）随着持续模糊测试的推进，代码覆盖率持续提升；（iii）覆盖率的变化有助于发现新的模糊测试漏洞；（iv）开发者提供的种子语料库具有长期有效性。本研究为理解持续模糊测试如何促进模糊测试漏洞的发现提供了实证依据，对未来持续模糊测试策略制定及工具开发具有重要的实践意义。

title: Explainable and Robust AI-Enhanced Secure Verification Using Adaptive Federated Learning and Theoretical Analysis
authors: Sivakumar Depuru, Nagooru Chandana, P. Alekhya, S. Madhu, Seela Venkata Chandana Devi
publish: 2026 Second International Conference on Multi-Agent Systems for Collaborative Intelligence (ICMSCI)
abstract: Modern secure verification systems are becoming more dependent on artificial intelligence to enhance both detection accuracy and automation, although the majority of systems today are unchanged and opaque and do not provide formal performance guarantees. To overcome the shortcomings, the research introduced a flexible and interpretable AIgrounded secure design that merges federated intelligence, trust-aware learning and theoretical authentication of privacypreserving verification settings. The suggested framework uses an adaptive learning process which dynamically is used to revise the model parameters amongst distributed nodes without breaching data confidentiality. To increase the transparency, an explainability module is also added to decode the rationale of decision, trust scores allowing the accountable and verifiable verification. Moreover, there are formal convergence and complexity analysis that introduces theoretical performance guarantees. A large scale of experiments on various benchmark data reveals that the proposed framework offers better verification performance with up to 96-98 percent accuracy and better robustness in adversarial environments and faster convergence than state of art approaches. The effectiveness and cross-dataset evaluations through ablation, scalability, and cross-dataset evaluations are also used to prove the effectiveness and generalization ability of the proposed system. Such findings confirm the framework as a trusted, scalable, and explicable platform of next-generation secure verification applications.
title_zh: 可解释且鲁棒的AI增强安全验证：基于自适应联邦学习与理论分析
abstract_zh: 现代安全验证系统正越来越依赖人工智能来提升检测的准确性和自动化水平，然而目前大多数系统仍保持不变、缺乏透明度，且无法提供形式化的性能保障。为克服这些不足，本研究提出了一种灵活且可解释的人工智能驱动安全设计，该设计融合了联邦智能、信任感知学习以及隐私保护验证环境下的理论化认证机制。所提出的框架采用自适应学习过程，在不泄露数据机密性的前提下，动态地在分布式节点间更新模型参数。为了增强系统的透明性，还引入了一个可解释性模块，用于解析决策依据和信任评分，从而实现可问责、可验证的验证过程。此外，该框架还进行了形式化的收敛性与复杂度分析，提供了理论上的性能保证。在多个基准数据集上进行的大规模实验表明，该方法在验证性能上表现优异，准确率最高可达96%-98%，在对抗性环境中的鲁棒性更强，且收敛速度优于现有最先进方法。通过消融实验、可扩展性分析及跨数据集评估，进一步验证了该系统在有效性与泛化能力方面的优势。这些发现证实，该框架是下一代安全验证应用中可信、可扩展且可解释的理想平台。

title: HKT-SmartAudit: Distilling Lightweight Models for Smart Contract Auditing
authors: Zhiyuan Wei, Jing Sun, Zijian Zhang, Zhe Hou, Xianhao Zhang, Meng Li, Yuqiang Sun, Daoyuan Wu, Yang Liu, Chunmiao Li, Mingchao Wan, Jin Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The rapid growth of blockchain technology has driven the widespread adoption of smart contracts; however, their inherent vulnerabilities have led to significant financial losses. Traditional auditing methods, while essential, struggle to keep pace with the increasing complexity and scale of smart contracts. Large language models (LLMs) offer promising capabilities for automating vulnerability detection, but their adoption is often limited by high computational costs. Although prior work has explored leveraging large models through agents or workflows, relatively little attention has been given to improving the performance of smaller, fine-tuned models—a critical factor for achieving both efficiency and data privacy. In this paper, we introduce HKT-SmartAudit, a framework for developing lightweight models optimized for smart contract auditing. It features a multi-stage knowledge distillation pipeline that integrates classical distillation, external domain knowledge, and reward-guided learning to transfer high-quality insights from large teacher models. A single-task learning strategy is employed to train compact student models that maintain high accuracy and robustness while significantly reducing computational overhead. Experimental results show that our distilled models outperform both commercial tools and larger models in detecting complex vulnerabilities and logical flaws, offering a practical, secure, and scalable solution for smart contract auditing. The source code is available in the GitHub repository<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>.
title_zh: HKT-SmartAudit：面向智能合约审计的轻量级模型提炼方法
abstract_zh: 区块链技术的快速发展推动了智能合约的广泛应用；然而，其固有的漏洞已导致重大财务损失。传统的审计方法虽然至关重要，但难以跟上智能合约日益增长的复杂性和规模。大型语言模型（LLMs）在自动化漏洞检测方面展现出巨大潜力，但其应用常受限于高昂的计算成本。尽管已有研究探索通过代理或工作流利用大模型，但对于提升小型微调模型性能的关注仍相对较少——而这正是实现高效性与数据隐私的关键因素。本文提出 HKT-SmartAudit，一个专为智能合约审计优化的轻量级模型开发框架。该框架采用多阶段知识蒸馏流程，融合经典蒸馏、外部领域知识以及基于奖励的学习机制，将大型教师模型中的高质量洞察有效迁移至小型学生模型。我们采用单任务学习策略训练紧凑的学生模型，在显著降低计算开销的同时，保持了高准确率和强鲁棒性。实验结果表明，我们的蒸馏模型在检测复杂漏洞和逻辑缺陷方面，优于现有的商业工具及更大规模的模型，为智能合约审计提供了一种实用、安全且可扩展的解决方案。源代码已发布于 GitHub 仓库<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>。
